content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — designflow-data-syncing

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first — it is the canonical guide. This file holds only
Claude Code-specific notes. For cross-repo infrastructure/runtime standards, see
[u2giants/albert-standards `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/blob/main/infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md)
and update it when Cloud Run, Cloud Build, domains, BFF routing, secrets/env,
storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating rules change.

## Memory and branch

- User memory: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`. Work only on `sandbox-albert` (and `develop` for syncing).
  Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into
  local `sandbox-albert`, push that to GitHub, then pull `sandbox-albert` back locally before
  editing. Never commit to or merge into `main`. Commit locally, push to
  `origin/sandbox-albert`, then ship via PR `sandbox-albert` → `develop`, updating or creating
  it after pushes.

## Ignore guidance

Use `.claudeignore` (mirrors `.cursorignore`). It excludes `node_modules/`, caches, logs,
`uploads/`, Playwright artifacts, and `.env*` from AI context.

## Allowed operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, `npm install`, `npm test`, `node`, `git add/commit`,
  `git push origin sandbox-albert`.
- Not allowed: editing `node_modules/`; `git push --force`; committing to `main`.

## Tests

- `npm test` (Jest, `tests/**/*.test.js`). Most `start:*` scripts run `npm test` first;
  `start:sandbox-albert` does **not**, so run it yourself before committing.
- Add/extend a unit test for any function you add or change.

## Deployment / SSH

Deployment is Cloud Build → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run (see `AGENTS.md` §7). The service is
private (`--no-allow-unauthenticated`). SSH is **not** part of the workflow.

## Commit style

```
type(scope): short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `chore`. Scope e.g. `sync`, `coldlion`, `migration`.

## Claude-specific behaviors

- Quote mixed-case identifiers in raw `ALTER TABLE` DDL (`models/db.js`) — see AGENTS.md §11.
- Never bulk-load the DB through a Windows console (CP437 mojibake risk) — see AGENTS.md §11/§12.
- Keep `models/db/` in sync with `designflow-backend` when changing schema (no shared package).

### Admin "Archive oldest RFQ items" dialog (2026-06-22)

**Looks like:** archiving is only per-row from the grid context menu.

**Actually:** there is also an admin-only bulk action — `ArchiveOldestDialogComponent`
(`pages/rfq/archive-oldest-dialog/`), opened from the RFQ toolbar `admin-group` (`role == 'admin'`).

`itemService.archiveOldestRFQItems({ limit, dryRun })` → backend `POST /archiveOldestRFQItems`.
On success it closes with `true` so the caller refreshes the grid. "Oldest" + reversibility are
defined server-side (see `designflow-backend` AGENTS §11).

**Future sessions should:** keep the preview-before-archive flow; the dialog is declared in
`rfq.module.ts`.

**No `shared-db` / Supabase update made** — and that's correct: this session's changes are all in the **DesignFlow** stack (its own Postgres on Cloud Run + the Node proxy + Angular). The archive feature touches DesignFlow's `RFQItem` table, not the Supabase shared DB; the BFF changes are Node proxy-layer only (I did not touch the `crm_api_*.sql` RLS files). No Supabase schema, RLS, migration, realtime, or API-contract changed, so there's nothing durable for `u2giants/shared-db`.

## Handoff
- `HANDOFF.md`: **present** — `designflow-backend/HANDOFF.md` (created) and `designflow-frontend/HANDOFF.md` (updated).
- Reason: backend has two genuinely unfinished items needing continuation — the **JWT auth-bypass fix** (blocked on developer sign-off; reminder scheduled) and the **Brevo key rotation** (manual, key still exposed in git history). Frontend handoff carries the three deferred performance items.

## Verification
- Docs verified against session findings: **yes** — every entry maps to code I changed or commands I ran (frontend `tsc` clean on my files + 200 specs; backend 9 archive tests; data-syncing 8 tests incl. new; bff 5 tests; the 98-module count from a live `require`).
- Secrets added: **no** — the Brevo key value lives only in 1Password; docs reference the item name and "rotate it," never the value.
- Unknowns remaining:
  - **External standards repo not updated:** the BFF (timeouts/OIDC/CORS/env) and backend (`BREVO_API_KEY`) changes fall under categories the repos' CLAUDE.md says to mirror in `u2giants/albert-standards` → `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`. Not done — that repo isn't checked out locally. Flagged in `designflow-bff/AGENTS.md`. **Verify/do:** clone it and add the BFF + secrets notes.
  - **`BREVO_API_KEY` wiring:** unverified whether it's actually provisioned to the backend Cloud Run service (it's in `.env.example` + the env table). Verify with `gcloud run services describe`.
  - **AG Grid trim candidate:** whether `rowGroup`/`pivot` colDef props are actually exercised at runtime is unconfirmed; the safe module list must be finalized via runtime `ValidationModule` discovery, not inspection (documented in `ag-grid-modules.ts`).

One note for your awareness: nothing this session has been committed — all changes (code + docs across the 4 repos) are uncommitted in their working trees on `sandbox-albert`. Say the word and I'll commit per-repo with appropriate messages.

content.text: `http.get` returns `Object` by default; my callbacks need `: any`. Let me fix:

input.command:
cd /c/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend
sed -i 's/\.subscribe((v) =>/.subscribe((v: any) =>/g' src/app/helpers/services/main.service.caching.spec.ts
grep -n "subscribe((v" src/app/helpers/services/main.service.caching.spec.ts

cd /c/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend

fix(proxy): timeouts, OIDC client cache, API-key parse cache, fail-closed CORS

- routes/api.js: real proxy timeouts (BFF_PROXY_TIMEOUT_MS, default 30s) instead of 0/0;
  remove dead err.response passthrough (proxy errors are handled by proxy.on("error")).

- src/apiKeyAuth.js: memoize parsed + hashed DESIGNFLOW_API_KEYS instead of per request.
- src/server.js: CORS fails closed — explicit CORS_ORIGINS allowlist required outside localhost.
- Docs: AGENTS.md §9 quirk + §10 env (BFF_PROXY_TIMEOUT_MS, CORS_ORIGINS behavior).

content.text:
All four repos are clean, synced with their remotes, and PR'd. Done.

## What I did, in your requested order

**1. Synced `develop` → `sandbox-albert` before finalizing.** I fetched each repo and merged the latest `origin/develop` into `sandbox-albert`. The only `develop` commits were automated `chore: sync shared-db` (vendored `shared-db/` files) — **zero conflicts** with anything I touched. One honest note: the session's code was already written when you gave this instruction, so I committed it and merged `develop` on top — the end state of `sandbox-albert` is `develop` + my work, which is what the workflow targets. Next session I'll do the develop-pull first, before writing code.

**2. Only touched `sandbox-albert` and read-only `develop`.** I merged `develop` *into* `sandbox-albert` (never modified `develop`), pushed only `sandbox-albert`, and based every PR on `develop`. `main` was never touched.

**3. Added unit tests for everything I created** (and ran them all green):
- backend `archiveOldestRfq.test.js` — 9
- data-syncing `externalVendorCustomer.test.js` — 7
- frontend `archive-oldest-dialog.component.spec.ts` — 8, `main.service.caching.spec.ts` — 6

**Committed, merged, pushed, PR'd — per repo:**

| Repo | Pushed | PR |
|---|---|---|
| designflow-data-syncing | ✅ | **created** [#13](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-data-syncing/pull/13) |
| designflow-backend | ✅ | **existing #44 updated** + [comment](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend/pull/44) |
| designflow-bff | ✅ | **created** [#13](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-bff/pull/13) |
| designflow-frontend | ✅ | **existing #124 updated** + [comment](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/124) |

Where a standing PR already existed, the push auto-updated it and I added a comment summarizing this session's commits (rather than duplicating the PR).

## Things to be aware of
- **CI will run on each PR.** Two known, pre-existing red spots that are **not** from my changes: frontend `rfq.component.spec.ts` (the `notesDataSource` GridOptions type error, documented in HANDOFF.md) and backend `rfqBulkImportTemplate.test.js` (locally missing `exceljs` — CI installs deps so it should pass there). My new/changed tests all pass.
- **Pushing triggers Cloud Build deploys** (per the repos' deploy docs) for each `sandbox-albert` push — expected given you asked to push.
- **Still open (in HANDOFF + PR notes, not done):** rotate the Brevo key, the JWT `algorithms` fix (reminder set for tomorrow), and the external `albert-standards` infra doc update (that repo isn't checked out locally).

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code notes for designflow-backend

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first. It is the canonical guide for repo structure, custom-code boundaries, data model, migrations, deployment, incidents, and pending work. This file is only for Claude Code-specific operating notes. For cross-repo infrastructure/runtime standards, see [u2giants/albert-standards `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/blob/main/infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md) and update it when Cloud Run, Cloud Build, domains, BFF routing, secrets/env, storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating rules change.

## Memory and Branch

- User memory file: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Commit and push only to `sandbox-albert`
- Merge or switch branches only when the user explicitly asks; normal delivery is PR to `develop`

## Ignore Guidance

Use `.claudeignore`. It matches `.cursorignore` and excludes generated, vendored, cache, Playwright, runtime upload, and large data paths such as `node_modules/`, `coverage/`, `dist/`, `.cache/`, `images/`, `thumbs/`, and `data/hts-rates.json`.

## Allowed Operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, run `npm` scripts, use `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push origin sandbox-albert`
- Not allowed by default: force-push, rewrite history, edit `node_modules/`
- SSH is not the normal deployment path. Deployment is Cloud Build -> Docker -> Cloud Run after branch push; use `gcloud` only for explicit rollback or incident triage.

## Tests

```powershell
npm test
npm test -- --testPathPattern="<name>"
```

Run the relevant tests before committing. `start:sandbox-albert` does not run tests before starting `nodemon`.

## Claude-Specific Rules

- For DB columns: model field + `models/db.js` inline migration + `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` assertion.
- Quote mixed-case Postgres column identifiers in inline migrations.
- Add code to `routes/lib.router.js` and `models/lib.model.js` surgically; they are large legacy files.
- Keep secrets out of `--set-env-vars`; use Secret Manager via `--set-secrets`.

## Commit Style

```text
type(scope): short description
```

Use `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, or `chore`. Keep messages specific.

content.text:
This is the migration I flagged as highest-risk, and reading `getAll` confirms the core challenge: the response is built by `utility.remapRFQItem(...)` — many grid columns (FOB Cost, Landed Cost, CBM/pc, Price for Sales, merch-group fields) are **computed in JS**, not DB columns. SSRM pushes sort/filter to SQL, so those computed columns can't be sorted/filtered server-side without special handling. That's a real design fork that changes the implementation.

Given this touches the most critical page and has genuine design decisions, I'm going to plan it properly (read-only) before writing code, rather than blindly implement hundreds of lines. Let me enter plan mode and explore thoroughly.

content.content:
Entered plan mode. You should now focus on exploring the codebase and designing an implementation approach.

In plan mode, you should:
1. Thoroughly explore the codebase to understand existing patterns
2. Identify similar features and architectural approaches
3. Consider multiple approaches and their trade-offs
4. Use AskUserQuestion if you need to clarify the approach
5. Design a concrete implementation strategy
6. When ready, use ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval

Remember: DO NOT write or edit any files yet. This is a read-only exploration and planning phase.

content.text: Following the planning workflow. Let me launch parallel Explore agents to map (1) the existing SSRM pattern to copy, (2) the current RFQ frontend grid in depth, and (3) the backend RFQ query and which columns are DB-backed vs computed.

input.prompt:
I'm planning a migration of the RFQ grid in C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend to AG Grid's server-side row model (SSRM). Other grids already use SSRM and I need the exact pattern to copy.

Map how the **itemLibrary** page implements SSRM end-to-end, and note any differences in **prod_tracking** and **licensing_tracking**:

FRONTEND (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\itemLibrary and pages\prod_tracking):
- The gridOptions: rowModelType, cacheBlockSize, serverSideDatasource/serverSideStoreType, pagination settings, maxBlocksInCache.
- The datasource object: the exact getRows(params) implementation — what it reads from params.request (startRow, endRow, sortModel, filterModel, groupKeys), how it calls the service, and how it calls params.success({ rowData, rowCount }) / params.fail().
- How server-side filtering and sorting are wired (are filters/sorts sent to the backend? which filter types — agSetColumnFilter, agTextColumnFilter, etc.?).
- Whether/how it does master/detail with SSRM (if any).

BACKEND: find the endpoint(s) the itemLibrary/prod_tracking datasource calls. itemLibrary likely hits designflow-item-master (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-item-master), prod_tracking likely designflow-tracking (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-tracking). For the main list endpoint(s):
- The exact request contract (startRow/endRow/sortModel/filterModel field names).
- How pagination (limit/offset), sorting (order), and filtering (where) are translated into Sequelize, especially how AG Grid filterModel is converted to Sequelize where clauses, and how sortModel becomes order.
- The response shape returned to the client (rows + total count / lastRow).
- How they get the total row count alongside a page (findAndCountAll? separate count?).

Report concrete code with file:line references (relative to C:\repos\dflow). Quote the key getRows() and the backend query-translation code. Be specific and exhaustive about the request/response contract — I need to replicate it exactly for RFQ. Return only findings, no preamble.

I'm planning to migrate the RFQ grid (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq) from AG Grid's client-side row model to the server-side row model (SSRM). I need an exhaustive map of how the RFQ grid currently works so the migration preserves all behavior.

Explore C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq (rfq.component.ts, rfq.component.html, the grid config in src\app\helpers\ag-grid\rfq-grid\rfq.grid.config.ts, detail-cell-renderer, autocomplete) and report:

1. **Grid setup / gridOptions**: rowModelType (confirm it's clientSide), gridOptions object, how rowData is set (getAllRFQItem → itemService.getAllItem → this.rowData), getRowId, and the loading flow. Quote the gridOptions and the getAllRFQItem method with line numbers.

2. **Columns — which are DB-backed vs computed**: go through RFQ_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG.columnDefs. For each column note: colId, field, and whether the value is (a) a plain field from the row, (b) a valueGetter/valueFormatter that computes from other fields (e.g. FOB Cost, Landed Cost, CBM/pc, Price for Sales, margins), or (c) a lookup. List which columns have sortable/filter set and what filter type (agSetColumnFilter / agTextColumnFilter / agNumberColumnFilter / agDateColumnFilter). This is critical: I need to know which columns CAN be sorted/filtered in SQL vs which are computed in JS.

3. **Master/detail**: how the detail grid (detail-cell-renderer) gets its data — does it read params.data.Vendors from the master row, or fetch separately? What master-row fields does the detail depend on? Quote the relevant agInit/rowData lines.

4. **Filtering & sorting today**: is it pure client-side AG Grid (no server round-trip)? Any default filters applied on load (e.g. default Step filter — search applyDefaultStepFilter)? Any quick filter / floating filters? Any postSortRows / comparators?

5. **Cross-cutting features that SSRM could break** (per AGENTS.md warnings): Saved Views (applyGridViewStateForViewName / column state), the Division logic (selectedDivisionCode/divisionStatus), export (onBtExport — does it export all rows or visible?), cell notes (notesDataSource), select-all / bulk operations, the highlightedItem behavior, undo/redo.

Report concrete code with file:line references relative to C:\repos\dflow. Be exhaustive on #2 and #3. Return only findings, no preamble.

I'm planning to add server-side pagination + server-side filtering/sorting to the RFQ list query in C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend. I need to understand the current query and what is DB-backed vs computed.

Explore C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend and report:

1. **Customer.getAll** in models/lib.model.js (around line 340-482): the full function. What request fields it reads (req.body.status, divisionCode, divisionStatus, highlightedItem, filterd_rfq), the where `condition` it builds (vendor vs internal role), `order`, `subQuery`, and the findAll with `attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES }` and `include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(...)`. Quote the computed ones. I need to know which grid columns map to a real DB column (sortable/filterable in SQL) vs computed-in-JS (cannot be sorted/filtered in SQL without replicating logic).

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the information I need. Let me create the comprehensive mapping document.

## RFQ Grid SSRM Migration Mapping

### 1. GRID SETUP / GRIDOPTIONS

**File:** `designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq\rfq.component.ts` (lines 222-375)

**rowModelType:** `clientSide` (implicit; no explicit `rowModelType` set in gridOptions, defaulting to CRSM)

**gridOptions object** (lines 222-375):
```typescript
this.gridOptions = {
  masterDetail: true,
  popupParent: document.body,
  loading: true,
  overlayLoadingTemplate: this.overlayLoadingTemplate,
  detailCellRenderer: 'myDetailCellRenderer',
  detailCellRendererParams: {
    detailGridOptions: {
      components: this.frameworkComponents,
      tooltipShowDelay: 1000,
      tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated',
      defaultColDef: {
        editable: true,
        resizable: true,
        sortable: true,
        filter: true
      }
    },
    getDetailRowData: (params) => {
      params.successCallback(params.data.Vendors || []);
    },
    onFactoriesAdded: (rowData: any) => this.advanceStepToFactoriesAfterFactoryAdd(rowData),
  },
  defaultColDef: {
    sortable: true,
    resizable: true,
    filter: 'agTextColumnFilter',
    floatingFilter: true,
    wrapHeaderText: true,
    autoHeaderHeight: true
  },
  rowGroupPanelShow: "always",
  groupDefaultExpanded: 1,
  autoGroupColumnDef: {
    sort: 'desc',
    comparator: (valueA: any, valueB: any, nodeA: any, nodeB: any) => {
      const maxDate = (node: any): number => {
        const leaves: any[] = node.childrenAfterFilter || node.allLeafChildren || [];
        let max = 0;
        for (const leaf of leaves) {
          if (leaf.group) {
            const sub = maxDate(leaf);
            if (sub > max) max = sub;
          } else {
            const d = leaf.data?.['Date Modified'];
            if (d) {
              const t = new Date(d).getTime();
              if (!isNaN(t) && t > max) max = t;
            }
          }
        }
        return max;
      };
      return maxDate(nodeA) - maxDate(nodeB);
    }
  },
  rowSelection: {
    mode: 'multiRow',
    checkboxes: true,
    checkboxLocation: 'autoGroupColumn',
    headerCheckbox: true,
    selectAll: 'filtered',
    enableClickSelection: false,
    groupSelects: 'descendants'
  },
  tooltipShowDelay: 1000,
  tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated',
  enableRowPinning: true,
  suppressHorizontalScroll: false,
  enableFilterHandlers: true,
  sideBar: this.sideBarConfig,
  statusBar: {
    statusPanels: [
      { statusPanel: 'customStatusBar', align: 'left' }
    ]
  },
  getContextMenuItems: this.getContextMenuItems.bind(this),
  getRowId: (params: GetRowIdParams) => params.data['Price Code'],
  notesDataSource: this.rfqCellNotes.dataSource,
  processCellForClipboard: this.processCellForClipboard.bind(this),
  processCellFromClipboard: this.processCellFromClipboard.bind(this),
  cellSelection: {
    handle: { mode: "fill", direction: 'y', setFillValue: this.handleSetFillValue.bind(this) },
    suppressMultiRanges: true
  },
  undoRedoCellEditing: true,
  undoRedoCellEditingLimit: 50,
  onFilterOpened: this.onFilterOpened.bind(this),
  onFilterChanged: this.onFilterChanged.bind(this),
  onFilterModified: this.onFilterModified.bind(this),
  onSelectionChanged: this.onSelectionChanged.bind(this),
  onCellValueChanged: this.onCellValueChanged.bind(this),
  onCellMouseOver: this.onCellMouseOver.bind(this),
  onCellEditingStarted: this.onCellEditingStarted.bind(this),
  onCellEditingStopped: this.onCellEditingStopped.bind(this),
  onFirstDataRendered: this.onFirstDataRendered.bind(this),
  onGridReady: this.onGridReady.bind(this),
  postSortRows: (params: any) => {
    const nodes: any[] = params.nodes;
    if (!nodes.length || !nodes[0].group) return;
    const maxDate = (node: any): number => {
      const leaves: any[] = node.childrenAfterFilter?.length
        ? node.childrenAfterFilter
        : (node.allLeafChildren || []);
      let max = 0;
      for (const leaf of leaves) {
        const d = leaf.data?.['Date Modified'];
        if (d) {
          const t = new Date(d).getTime();
          if (!isNaN(t) && t > max) max = t;
        }
      }
      return max;
    };
    nodes.sort((a: any, b: any) => maxDate(b) - maxDate(a));
  },
  onColumnVisible: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onColumnPinned: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onColumnResized: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onColumnMoved: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onSortChanged: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  components: this.frameworkComponents
};
```

**Row data loading** (lines 956-2047):
- `getAllRFQItem()` calls `itemService.getAllItem({ status, divisionCode, divisionStatus, highlightedItem, filterd_rfq })`
- Returns full array, mapped through `prepareRowData()` (line 2015)
- Set to grid via `this.gridApi.setGridOption('rowData', enrichedData)` (line 2023)

**getRowId** (line 310): `params.data['Price Code']` — each row's unique identifier

**Loading flow:**
1. `ngOnInit()` → `loadInitialMetadata()` (forkJoin of 9 parallel calls: role, company, status, rfqGroup, customers, delivery, steps, licenses, cbm) (lines 659-726)
2. `afterMetadataReady()` → `getAllRFQItem()` (line 800)
3. Grid receives full rowData array, preloads cell notes, then applies saved view state (lines 2015-2036)
4. `onFirstDataRendered()` applies default Step filter, then master-detail expansion (line 4003-4014)

---

### 2. COLUMNS — DB-BACKED VS COMPUTED

**Column config file:** `designflow-frontend\src\app\helpers\ag-grid\rfq-grid\rfq.grid.config.ts` (lines 1-162)

**Column definitions from RFQ_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG.columnDefs** (all via `addColumn()` helper):

| ColId | Field | Type | Sortable | Filter Type | Notes |
|-------|-------|------|----------|-------------|-------|
| `price_code` | "Price Code" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Pinned left, agGroupCellRenderer, not editable |
| `pic` | "Picture" | DB | No | No | sortable: false |
| `desc` | "Description" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Custom text-matcher filter (lines 1007-1023) |
| `rfq_group` | "RFQ Group" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | rowGroup: true |
| `item_label` | "Item Label" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Hidden |
| `date_mod` | "Date Modified" | DB | Yes | agDateColumnFilter | Custom comparator, valueGetter, filterParams (lines 1458-1544) |
| `step` | "Step" | DB | Yes | agSetColumnFilter | Custom filterParams comparator for step ordering (lines 72-88 in config) |
| `factory_updated` | "Factory_updated" | DB | Yes | agDateColumnFilter | Non-editable, custom valueGetter/comparator (lines 1546-1580) |
| `group` | "Group" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete editor |
| `material` | "Product Type" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete editor, valueGetter/valueSetter (lines 1301-1330) |
| `construction` | "Product Sub-Type" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete editor, valueGetter/valueSetter (lines 1331-1361) |
| `treatment` | "Product Sub-Sub-Type" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete editor, valueGetter/valueSetter (lines 1367-1397) |
| `tech_pack_url` | "Tech Pack Link" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Custom cellRenderer (lines 1449-1457) |
| `size` | "Size (LxW)" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete editor, valueGetter/valueSetter (lines 1102-1127) |
| `depth` | "Depth" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete editor, valueGetter/valueSetter (lines 1127-1152) |
| `case_pack` | "Case Pack" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Custom valueSetter guards vendor quotation state (lines 1078-1101) |
| `quantity` | "Quantity" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Custom valueSetter guards vendor response count (lines 993-1004) |
| `customer` | "Customer" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete, valueGetter/valueSetter sets dilution/logistics_load side-effects (lines 1152-1182) |
| `license` | "License" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete, single select, triggers royalty_rate lookup (lines 1183-1202) |
| `delivery_location` | "Delivery Location" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete, async valueSetter (lines 1203-1214) |
| `comp_retail` | "Comp Retail" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | cellDataType: false |
| `assumed_wholesale` | "Assumed Wholesale" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, cellDataType: false |
| `fob_cost` | "FOB Cost" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, async valueSetter triggers recalculate_Lic_Gen (lines 1581-1591) |
| `duty_rate` | "Duty Rate %" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | **COMPUTED** — custom cellRenderer with duty_rate_dollar_amount superscript, math expr editor, equation storage (lines 1622-1686) |
| `freight` | "Freight $" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, async valueSetter triggers recalculate_Lic_Gen (lines 1714-1723) |
| `landed_cost` | "Landed Cost $" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | **COMPUTED** via valueGetter: calculateLandedCost(FOB + agent + duty + freight) (lines 1043-1077); manual override via valueSetter + flag |
| `is_landed_cost_manual` | "Is Landed Cost Manual" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Hidden, boolean flag for landed cost override |
| `container_price` | "Container Price" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, async valueSetter recalcs freight/landed cost (lines 1724-1758) |
| `dilution` | "Dilution %" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, async valueSetter → recalculate (lines 1769-1778) |
| `logistics_load` | "Logistics Load %" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, async valueSetter → recalculate (lines 1759-1768) |
| `cbm_pc` | "CBM/pc" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, custom comparator, async valueSetter recalcs freight/landed (lines 1592-1621) |
| `agent` | "Agent" | DB (likely hidden/admin) | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | NumericEditor, async valueSetter → recalculate_Lic_Gen (lines 1687-1695) |
| `carton_length` | "Carton length" | **COMPUTED** | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | valueGetter reads matched vendor's carton_length from Vendors array (lines 1696-1701) |
| `carton_width` | "Carton width" | **COMPUTED** | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | valueGetter reads matched vendor's carton_width from Vendors array, hidden (lines 1702-1707) |
| `carton_height` | "Carton height" | **COMPUTED** | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | valueGetter reads matched vendor's carton_height from Vendors array, hidden (lines 1708-1713) |
| `res` | "Res" (response count) | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Response ratio like "3/5" |
| `quote` | "#Quote" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Likely derived |
| `chosen_vendor` | "Chosen Vendor" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete, valueGetter/valueSetter (lines 1258-1276) |
| `div` | "Div" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete, valueGetter/valueSetter (lines 1426-1447), hidden |
| `status` | "Status" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Autocomplete, cellRenderer with tooltip for blocking rules, async valueSetter validates tech pack + required fields (lines 1215-1242) |
| `note` | "Notes" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Integrated with notesDataSource (AG Grid cell notes) |
| `adam_fix` | "adam_fix" | DB | Yes | agMultiColumnFilter | Hidden, admin? |
| **Gen Price for Sales** (8 computed)| | | | | |
| `gen_fob_price_sales` | "Gen FOB Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer, tooltip with duty/container snapshot |
| `gen_poe_price_sales` | "Gen POE Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer |
| `gen_whse_price_sales` | "Gen Whse Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer |
| `gen_mddp_price_sales` | "Gen MDDP Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer |
| `lic_fob_price_sales` | "Lic FOB Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer |
| `lic_poe_price_sales` | "Lic POE Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer |
| `lic_mddp_price_sales` | "Lic MDDP Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer |
| `lic_whse_price_sales` | "Lic Whse Price for Sales" | **COMPUTED** | No filter? | currency fmt, snapshot renderer |
| **Gen/Lic Margins & Sell Prices** (16 total) | | **COMPUTED** via equation.ts methods | No explicit filter | Triggers recalculate_Lic_Gen on valueSetter | NumericEditor, async valueSetter |

**Key computed columns requiring JS recalculation:**
- **Landed Cost**: `(FOB * (1 + Agent%) * (1 + Duty%) + DutyDollar + Freight)` (line 2082-2093)
- **Duty Rate**: can be equation (e.g., "5.3+25+10") or simple %, stored in DB as `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (lines 1622-1686)
- **Gen/Lic Price for Sales margins & prices**: driven by 8 custom equation methods (setGenFobMargin, setGenFobSellPrice, setLicFobMargin, etc.) called via valueSetter (lines 1781-1983)
- **Carton dimensions**: read from matched vendor in Vendors array via chosenVendor lookup (lines 1696-1713)

**Filters summary:**
- Most columns: `agTextColumnFilter` or `agMultiColumnFilter` (default, lines 249-253)
- Step: `agSetColumnFilter` with custom comparator for step order (lines 72-88)
- Date columns (date_mod, factory_updated): `agDateColumnFilter` with custom browser picker (lines 1458-1544, 1549-1580)

---

### 3. MASTER/DETAIL

**Detail grid data loading:**
- **Master-detail config** (lines 223-247):
  ```typescript
  masterDetail: true,
  detailCellRenderer: 'myDetailCellRenderer',
  detailCellRendererParams: {
    getDetailRowData: (params) => {
      params.successCallback(params.data.Vendors || []);
    },
    onFactoriesAdded: (rowData: any) => this.advanceStepToFactoriesAfterFactoryAdd(rowData),
  }
  ```

- **Detail grid component:** `designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq\detail-cell-renderer\detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` (lines 105-148)
  - `agInit(params)`: receives master row as `params` (line 116)
  - `this.rowData = params.data.Vendors` (line 147) — **detail grid reads Vendors array directly from master row data**
  - No separate fetch; Vendors are embedded in each master row returned by `getAllItem()`

- **Detail grid columns** are configured in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`:
  - Loads from `RFQ_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG` (line 126)
  - Filters columns by role (line 127)
  - Configures detail-specific headers via `loadDetailColumnHeaders()` / `configureDetailColumnHeaders()` (lines 136-138)

- **Master row fields depended on by detail:**
  - `Vendors`: array of vendor quotations (line 147)
  - `'Chosen Vendor'`: used to match vendor dimensions in carton_length/width/height valueGetters (lines 1698-1712)
  - `'FOB Country'`: pulled from matched vendor row for duty rate dialog (line 2242)
  - Any field read by detail's custom equation/editor functions

---

### 4. FILTERING & SORTING TODAY

**Pure client-side AG Grid (no server round-trip for filter/sort):**
- Row data loaded once in `getAllRFQItem()` (line 956)
- All subsequent filter/sort operations on `rowData` array via gridApi methods

**Default filters on load:**
- **Step filter** (lines 4243-4264): applied in `applyDefaultStepFilter()` called from `onFirstDataRendered()` (line 4007)
  - Admin/production: `['@sourcing', '@factories']` (filterType: 'set')
  - Designer: `[null]` (blank step only)
  - Other roles: no filter
  - Code: `this.gridApi.setColumnFilterModel('step', { filterType: 'set', values: filterValue })`

**Filter event handlers:**
- `onFilterOpened()` (line 329) — no-op in code
- `onFilterChanged()` (lines 330, 3767-3769) — saves grid state to sessionStorage
- `onFilterModified()` (lines 331, 3829-3833) — reads filter model

**Quick filter / floating filters:**
- `floatingFilter: true` in defaultColDef (line 253)

**postSortRows:**
- Custom group-row sort by Date Modified (descending) — most recently modified items first (lines 344-364)
- Overrides user click sorts to always group by recency, not user's chosen column

**Division logic:**
- `selectedDivisionCode` & `selectedDivisionCodeStatus` drive `getAllItem()` params (line 962)
- Changes trigger full data reload via `onDivisionCodeChange()` / `onDivisionCStatusChange()` (lines 4045-4131)
- Columns hidden/shown per division (Gen vs Lic pricing sections) (lines 4052-4103)

**Saved Views:**
- `applyGridViewStateForViewName()` (lines 501-558) loads saved column state + filter model from DB
- Restores via `gridApi.applyColumnState()` + `setFilterModel()` (lines 519, 526)
- Default Step filter re-applied if saved view has no 'step' key (lines 568-586)

**Session state persistence:**
- Grid state (columns, filters, timestamps) saved to `sessionStorage['gridState_rfq']` on filter/column changes (lines 3771-3792)
- Restored on page reload via `restoreStateFromSession()` (lines 3794-3828)
- Hard reset flag `sessionStorage['rfq_hard_reset_pending']` can wipe state (lines 3796-3799)

**Cell highlighting (highlightedItem):**
- URL param parsing in `verifyVendorMode()` (lines 3978-4000) extracts highlighted item codes
- Row styles applied via `getRowStyle` callback (lines 3992-3998)
- Passed to `getAllItem()` as `{ highlightedItem, filterd_rfq }` (line 962)

---

### 5. CROSS-CUTTING FEATURES THAT SSRM COULD BREAK

**A. Saved Views (applyGridViewStateForViewName)**
- **Location:** lines 501-558 (method), stored in DB via `itemService.saveGridViewState()` / `getGridViewState()`
- **Behavior:** Saves/restores columnState + filterModel
- **SSRM risk:** If column state encodes filtered rowData indices or row IDs that change on server fetch, restore could mismatch
- **Mitigation:** Ensure server persists/returns rows in same order, or use stable row IDs (currently `'Price Code'`, line 310)

**B. Division Logic (selectedDivisionCode/divisionStatus)**
- **Location:** lines 4045-4131 (`onDivisionCodeChange`, `onDivisionCStatusChange`)
- **Behavior:** 
  - Toggles Gen vs Lic column visibility per division (lines 4052-4103)
  - Triggers full `getAllItem()` reload with new divisionCode/divisionStatus params
- **SSRM risk:** Server must filter/return rows matching selected divisions; column visibility must match server's returned schema
- **Mitigation:** Pass division filters to server in SSRM getRows request

**C. Export (onBtExport)**
- **Location:** lines 2983-3001
- **Current behavior:** Exports `this.rowData.length` rows (line 2988) — **all visible rows after client-side filter**
- **SSRM risk:** With pagination, will only export visible/fetched page, not total dataset
- **Mitigation:** Export endpoint must fetch all matching rows or use server-side export; warn user about row count limit

**D. Cell Notes (notesDataSource)**
- **Location:** lines 211-220 (rfqCellNotes setup), passed to gridOptions (line 311)
- **Behavior:** 
  - `this.rfqCellNotes = this.agGridCellNotesService.createDataSource(...)` with `persistRowId: ({ rowData }) => rowData?.['id']`
  - Preloaded via `preloadCellNotes(...)` (line 2024) after rowData set
- **SSRM risk:** Cell notes keyed by row.id; must ensure server-fetched rows have consistent id field
- **Mitigation:** Server must return id field; preload notes for initial fetch + any paginated fetch

**E. Select-all / Bulk Operations**
- **Location:** 
  - Select-all checkbox: `selectAll: 'filtered'` (line 294) — selects all visible rows after filter
  - `getSelectedRfqRows()` (lines 2822-2841)
  - `onBulkAddFactories()` (lines 2844-2980), `onApplyBulkStatus()` (lines 2576-2632)
  - `onReminder()` (lines 4287-4335)
- **SSRM risk:** `selectAll: 'filtered'` will only select fetched page rows, not all matching server rows
- **Mitigation:** Disable `selectAll` or switch to row selection without "select all filtered" for SSRM; bulk ops work row-by-row on selected rows only (safe)

**F. Highlighted Item (highlightedItem)**
- **Location:** lines 3978-4000 (`verifyVendorMode`), line 962 (passed to getAllItem), line 3986-3998 (getRowStyle)
- **Behavior:** URL param `/filterd_rfq/{itemCodes}/true` highlights matching rows with background color
- **SSRM risk:** Server must return matching rows; client-side highlighting still works if rows are fetched
- **Mitigation:** Server-side filtering for highlighted items; pass itemCodes to SSRM getRows

**G. Undo/Redo**
- **Location:** lines 325-326 (`undoRedoCellEditing: true, undoRedoCellEditingLimit: 50`), line 3973-3975 (`undo()`)
- **Behavior:** AG Grid's built-in cell edit undo/redo (client memory)
- **SSRM risk:** None directly; undo/redo applies to in-memory edits before server persist
- **Mitigation:** No change needed; undo only affects unsaved edits

**H. postSortRows (Date Modified sorting for groups)**
- **Location:** lines 344-364
- **Behavior:** Sorts group nodes by max Date Modified of their children, always newest first
- **SSRM risk:** Server must return rows pre-sorted by Date Modified, or client postSortRows must re-sort after fetch
- **Mitigation:** Keep postSortRows; if server returns pre-sorted, it still works (idempotent)

**I. Master-Detail Expansion Defaults**
- **Location:** lines 4021-4034 (`applyRfqMasterDetailExpansionDefaults`)
- **Behavior:** Expands top-level group rows, collapses master rows + nested groups
- **SSRM risk:** Must run after every filter/sort change (server fetch); detail grid must lazy-load vendors
- **Mitigation:** Keep expansion logic; ensure detail getDetailRowData is async (line 243-245 is already synchronous reading Vendors array — may need async fetch if not lazy-loaded)

---

### 6. EQUATION & COMPUTED COLUMNS

**Price equation system:** `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts`
- **Triggered by:** margin/sell price/royalty cell edits via valueSetter (lines 1781-1983 for Gen/Lic columns)
- **Methods:** `recalculate_Lic_Gen()`, `setGenFobMargin()`, `setLicFobMargin()`, etc.
- **SSRM risk:** Equations run in JS after edit, results saved to DB via `updateRfqItem()`. All computed columns must be recalculated on server if row refreshed from DB.
- **Mitigation:** Ensure server's getRows returns pre-calculated values OR client always recalculates after edit

---

### 7. ONFILTERCHANGED BEHAVIOR

**Code:** lines 3767-3769, 330
```typescript
onFilterChanged(e) {
  this.saveStateToSession();
}
```
- Trivial: just persists filter state to sessionStorage
- No side-effects on grid behavior
- SSRM risk: none

---

## Summary for SSRM Migration

**Critical changes required:**
1. Add `serverSideDatasource` to gridOptions (replaces `rowData` array)
2. Implement IServerSideDatasource with `getRows()` that calls backend for filtered/sorted data
3. **Detail grid:** Ensure Vendors array is fetched or lazy-loaded (currently embedded in master row)
4. **Division filters:** Pass `selectedDivisionCode` & `selectedDivisionCodeStatus` to server in every getRows call
5. **Default Step filter:** Apply server-side via initial filterModel, or reapply client-side after server fetch
6. **Saved Views:** Column state unaffected; filter model saved/restored, but ensure rows returned in same order
7. **Export:** Warn user or implement server-side export for full datasets
8. **Highlighted items:** Server must filter rows matching highlighted item codes
9. **Carton dimensions, landed cost, equations:** Either pre-compute on server or trigger client recalc after edit
10. **Undo/redo:** May break if row data is refreshed from server mid-undo-chain; consider disabling or clearing undo on server sync

---

**Files to modify:**
- `rfq.component.ts`: Add serverSideDatasource, remove rowData array population, adjust getRows params
- `rfq.grid.config.ts`: Remove client-side computed columns or mark as server-cached
- Backend API: Implement paginated getRows endpoint with filters, sorting, division params
- `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`: Ensure Vendors array is available (may need separate fetch)

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the information I need. Let me create the comprehensive mapping document.

## RFQ Grid SSRM Migration Mapping

### 1. GRID SETUP / GRIDOPTIONS

**File:** `designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq\rfq.component.ts` (lines 222-375)

**rowModelType:** `clientSide` (implicit; no explicit `rowModelType` set in gridOptions, defaulting to CRSM)

**gridOptions object** (lines 222-375):
```typescript
this.gridOptions = {
  masterDetail: true,
  popupParent: document.body,
  loading: true,
  overlayLoadingTemplate: this.overlayLoadingTemplate,
  detailCellRenderer: 'myDetailCellRenderer',
  detailCellRendererParams: {
    detailGridOptions: {
      components: this.frameworkComponents,
      tooltipShowDelay: 1000,
      tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated',
      defaultColDef: {
        editable: true,
        resizable: true,
        sortable: true,
        filter: true
      }
    },
    getDetailRowData: (params) => {
      params.successCallback(params.data.Vendors || []);
    },
    onFactoriesAdded: (rowData: any) => this.advanceStepToFactoriesAfterFactoryAdd(rowData),
  },
  defaultColDef: {
    sortable: true,
    resizable: true,
    filter: 'agTextColumnFilter',
    floatingFilter: true,
    wrapHeaderText: true,
    autoHeaderHeight: true
  },
  rowGroupPanelShow: "always",
  groupDefaultExpanded: 1,
  autoGroupColumnDef: {
    sort: 'desc',
    comparator: (valueA: any, valueB: any, nodeA: any, nodeB: any) => {
      const maxDate = (node: any): number => {
        const leaves: any[] = node.childrenAfterFilter || node.allLeafChildren || [];
        let max = 0;
        for (const leaf of leaves) {
          if (leaf.group) {
            const sub = maxDate(leaf);
            if (sub > max) max = sub;
          } else {
            const d = leaf.data?.['Date Modified'];
            if (d) {
              const t = new Date(d).getTime();
              if (!isNaN(t) && t > max) max = t;
            }
          }
        }
        return max;
      };
      return maxDate(nodeA) - maxDate(nodeB);
    }
  },
  rowSelection: {
    mode: 'multiRow',
    checkboxes: true,
    checkboxLocation: 'autoGroupColumn',
    headerCheckbox: true,
    selectAll: 'filtered',
    enableClickSelection: false,
    groupSelects: 'descendants'
  },
  tooltipShowDelay: 1000,
  tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated',
  enableRowPinning: true,
  suppressHorizontalScroll: false,
  enableFilterHandlers: true,
  sideBar: this.sideBarConfig,
  statusBar: {
    statusPanels: [
      { statusPanel: 'customStatusBar', align: 'left' }
    ]
  },
  getContextMenuItems: this.getContextMenuItems.bind(this),
  getRowId: (params: GetRowIdParams) => params.data['Price Code'],
  notesDataSource: this.rfqCellNotes.dataSource,
  processCellForClipboard: this.processCellForClipboard.bind(this),
  processCellFromClipboard: this.processCellFromClipboard.bind(this),
  cellSelection: {
    handle: { mode: "fill", direction: 'y', setFillValue: this.handleSetFillValue.bind(this) },
    suppressMultiRanges: true
  },
  undoRedoCellEditing: true,
  undoRedoCellEditingLimit: 50,
  onFilterOpened: this.onFilterOpened.bind(this),
  onFilterChanged: this.onFilterChanged.bind(this),
  onFilterModified: this.onFilterModified.bind(this),
  onSelectionChanged: this.onSelectionChanged.bind(this),
  onCellValueChanged: this.onCellValueChanged.bind(this),
  onCellMouseOver: this.onCellMouseOver.bind(this),
  onCellEditingStarted: this.onCellEditingStarted.bind(this),
  onCellEditingStopped: this.onCellEditingStopped.bind(this),
  onFirstDataRendered: this.onFirstDataRendered.bind(this),
  onGridReady: this.onGridReady.bind(this),
  postSortRows: (params: any) => {
    const nodes: any[] = params.nodes;
    if (!nodes.length || !nodes[0].group) return;
    const maxDate = (node: any): number => {
      const leaves: any[] = node.childrenAfterFilter?.length
        ? node.childrenAfterFilter
        : (node.allLeafChildren || []);
      let max = 0;
      for (const leaf of leaves) {
        const d = leaf.data?.['Date Modified'];
        if (d) {
          const t = new Date(d).getTime();
          if (!isNaN(t) && t > max) max = t;
        }
      }
      return max;
    };
    nodes.sort((a: any, b: any) => maxDate(b) - maxDate(a));
  },
  onColumnVisible: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onColumnPinned: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onColumnResized: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onColumnMoved: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  onSortChanged: this.saveStateToSession.bind(this),
  components: this.frameworkComponents
};
```

**Row data loading** (lines 956-2047):
- `getAllRFQItem()` calls `itemService.getAllItem({ status, divisionCode, divisionStatus, highlightedItem, filterd_rfq })`
- Returns full array, mapped through `prepareRowData()` (line 2015)
- Set to grid via `this.gridApi.setGridOption('rowData', enrichedData)` (line 2023)

**getRowId** (line 310): `params.data['Price Code']` — each row's unique identifier

**Loading flow:**
1. `ngOnInit()` → `loadInitialMetadata()` (forkJoin of 9 parallel calls: role, company, status, rfqGroup, customers, delivery, steps, licenses, cbm) (lines 659-726)
2. `afterMetadataReady()` → `getAllRFQItem()` (line 800)
3. Grid receives full rowData array, preloads cell notes, then applies saved view state (lines 2015-2036)
4. `onFirstDataRendered()` applies default Step filter, then master-detail expansion (line 4003-4014)

---

### 2. COLUMNS — DB-BACKED VS COMPUTED

**Column config file:** `designflow-frontend\src\app\helpers\ag-grid\rfq-grid\rfq.grid.config.ts` (lines 1-162)

**Key computed columns requiring JS recalculation:**
- **Landed Cost**: `(FOB * (1 + Agent%) * (1 + Duty%) + DutyDollar + Freight)` (line 2082-2093)

- **Gen/Lic Price for Sales margins & prices**: driven by 8 custom equation methods (setGenFobMargin, setGenFobSellPrice, setLicFobMargin, etc.) called via valueSetter (lines 1781-1983)
- **Carton dimensions**: read from matched vendor in Vendors array via chosenVendor lookup (lines 1696-1713)

**Filters summary:**
- Most columns: `agTextColumnFilter` or `agMultiColumnFilter` (default, lines 249-253)
- Step: `agSetColumnFilter` with custom comparator for step order (lines 72-88)
- Date columns (date_mod, factory_updated): `agDateColumnFilter` with custom browser picker (lines 1458-1544, 1549-1580)

---

### 3. MASTER/DETAIL

**Detail grid data loading:**
- **Master-detail config** (lines 223-247):
  ```typescript
  masterDetail: true,
  detailCellRenderer: 'myDetailCellRenderer',
  detailCellRendererParams: {
    getDetailRowData: (params) => {
      params.successCallback(params.data.Vendors || []);
    },
    onFactoriesAdded: (rowData: any) => this.advanceStepToFactoriesAfterFactoryAdd(rowData),
  }
  ```

- **Detail grid component:** `designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq\detail-cell-renderer\detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` (lines 105-148)
  - `agInit(params)`: receives master row as `params` (line 116)
  - `this.rowData = params.data.Vendors` (line 147) — **detail grid reads Vendors array directly from master row data**
  - No separate fetch; Vendors are embedded in each master row returned by `getAllItem()`

- **Detail grid columns** are configured in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`:
  - Loads from `RFQ_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG` (line 126)
  - Filters columns by role (line 127)
  - Configures detail-specific headers via `loadDetailColumnHeaders()` / `configureDetailColumnHeaders()` (lines 136-138)

- **Master row fields depended on by detail:**
  - `Vendors`: array of vendor quotations (line 147)
  - `'Chosen Vendor'`: used to match vendor dimensions in carton_length/width/height valueGetters (lines 1698-1712)
  - `'FOB Country'`: pulled from matched vendor row for duty rate dialog (line 2242)
  - Any field read by detail's custom equation/editor functions

---

### 4. FILTERING & SORTING TODAY

**Pure client-side AG Grid (no server round-trip for filter/sort):**
- Row data loaded once in `getAllRFQItem()` (line 956)
- All subsequent filter/sort operations on `rowData` array via gridApi methods

**Default filters on load:**
- **Step filter** (lines 4243-4264): applied in `applyDefaultStepFilter()` called from `onFirstDataRendered()` (line 4007)
  - Admin/production: `['@sourcing', '@factories']` (filterType: 'set')
  - Designer: `[null]` (blank step only)
  - Other roles: no filter
  - Code: `this.gridApi.setColumnFilterModel('step', { filterType: 'set', values: filterValue })`

**Filter event handlers:**
- `onFilterOpened()` (line 329) — no-op in code
- `onFilterChanged()` (lines 330, 3767-3769) — saves grid state to sessionStorage
- `onFilterModified()` (lines 331, 3829-3833) — reads filter model

**Quick filter / floating filters:**
- `floatingFilter: true` in defaultColDef (line 253)

**postSortRows:**
- Custom group-row sort by Date Modified (descending) — most recently modified items first (lines 344-364)
- Overrides user click sorts to always group by recency, not user's chosen column

**Division logic:**
- `selectedDivisionCode` & `selectedDivisionCodeStatus` drive `getAllItem()` params (line 962)
- Changes trigger full data reload via `onDivisionCodeChange()` / `onDivisionCStatusChange()` (lines 4045-4131)
- Columns hidden/shown per division (Gen vs Lic pricing sections) (lines 4052-4103)

**Saved Views:**
- `applyGridViewStateForViewName()` (lines 501-558) loads saved column state + filter model from DB
- Restores via `gridApi.applyColumnState()` + `setFilterModel()` (lines 519, 526)
- Default Step filter re-applied if saved view has no 'step' key (lines 568-586)

**Session state persistence:**
- Grid state (columns, filters, timestamps) saved to `sessionStorage['gridState_rfq']` on filter/column changes (lines 3771-3792)
- Restored on page reload via `restoreStateFromSession()` (lines 3794-3828)
- Hard reset flag `sessionStorage['rfq_hard_reset_pending']` can wipe state (lines 3796-3799)

**Cell highlighting (highlightedItem):**
- URL param parsing in `verifyVendorMode()` (lines 3978-4000) extracts highlighted item codes
- Row styles applied via `getRowStyle` callback (lines 3992-3998)
- Passed to `getAllItem()` as `{ highlightedItem, filterd_rfq }` (line 962)

---

### 5. CROSS-CUTTING FEATURES THAT SSRM COULD BREAK

**A. Saved Views (applyGridViewStateForViewName)**
- **Location:** lines 501-558 (method), stored in DB via `itemService.saveGridViewState()` / `getGridViewState()`
- **Behavior:** Saves/restores columnState + filterModel
- **SSRM risk:** If column state encodes filtered rowData indices or row IDs that change on server fetch, restore could mismatch
- **Mitigation:** Ensure server persists/returns rows in same order, or use stable row IDs (currently `'Price Code'`, line 310)

**B. Division Logic (selectedDivisionCode/divisionStatus)**
- **Location:** lines 4045-4131 (`onDivisionCodeChange`, `onDivisionCStatusChange`)
- **Behavior:** 
  - Toggles Gen vs Lic column visibility per division (lines 4052-4103)
  - Triggers full `getAllItem()` reload with new divisionCode/divisionStatus params
- **SSRM risk:** Server must filter/return rows matching selected divisions; column visibility must match server's returned schema
- **Mitigation:** Pass division filters to server in SSRM getRows request

**C. Export (onBtExport)**
- **Location:** lines 2983-3001
- **Current behavior:** Exports `this.rowData.length` rows (line 2988) — **all visible rows after client-side filter**
- **SSRM risk:** With pagination, will only export visible/fetched page, not total dataset
- **Mitigation:** Export endpoint must fetch all matching rows or use server-side export; warn user about row count limit

**D. Cell Notes (notesDataSource)**
- **Location:** lines 211-220 (rfqCellNotes setup), passed to gridOptions (line 311)
- **Behavior:** 
  - `this.rfqCellNotes = this.agGridCellNotesService.createDataSource(...)` with `persistRowId: ({ rowData }) => rowData?.['id']`
  - Preloaded via `preloadCellNotes(...)` (line 2024) after rowData set
- **SSRM risk:** Cell notes keyed by row.id; must ensure server-fetched rows have consistent id field
- **Mitigation:** Server must return id field; preload notes for initial fetch + any paginated fetch

**E. Select-all / Bulk Operations**
- **Location:** 
  - Select-all checkbox: `selectAll: 'filtered'` (line 294) — selects all visible rows after filter
  - `getSelectedRfqRows()` (lines 2822-2841)
  - `onBulkAddFactories()` (lines 2844-2980), `onApplyBulkStatus()` (lines 2576-2632)
  - `onReminder()` (lines 4287-4335)
- **SSRM risk:** `selectAll: 'filtered'` will only select fetched page rows, not all matching server rows
- **Mitigation:** Disable `selectAll` or switch to row selection without "select all filtered" for SSRM; bulk ops work row-by-row on selected rows only (safe)

**F. Highlighted Item (highlightedItem)**
- **Location:** lines 3978-4000 (`verifyVendorMode`), line 962 (passed to getAllItem), line 3986-3998 (getRowStyle)
- **Behavior:** URL param `/filterd_rfq/{itemCodes}/true` highlights matching rows with background color
- **SSRM risk:** Server must return matching rows; client-side highlighting still works if rows are fetched
- **Mitigation:** Server-side filtering for highlighted items; pass itemCodes to SSRM getRows

**G. Undo/Redo**
- **Location:** lines 325-326 (`undoRedoCellEditing: true, undoRedoCellEditingLimit: 50`), line 3973-3975 (`undo()`)
- **Behavior:** AG Grid's built-in cell edit undo/redo (client memory)
- **SSRM risk:** None directly; undo/redo applies to in-memory edits before server persist
- **Mitigation:** No change needed; undo only affects unsaved edits

**H. postSortRows (Date Modified sorting for groups)**
- **Location:** lines 344-364
- **Behavior:** Sorts group nodes by max Date Modified of their children, always newest first
- **SSRM risk:** Server must return rows pre-sorted by Date Modified, or client postSortRows must re-sort after fetch
- **Mitigation:** Keep postSortRows; if server returns pre-sorted, it still works (idempotent)

**I. Master-Detail Expansion Defaults**
- **Location:** lines 4021-4034 (`applyRfqMasterDetailExpansionDefaults`)
- **Behavior:** Expands top-level group rows, collapses master rows + nested groups
- **SSRM risk:** Must run after every filter/sort change (server fetch); detail grid must lazy-load vendors
- **Mitigation:** Keep expansion logic; ensure detail getDetailRowData is async (line 243-245 is already synchronous reading Vendors array — may need async fetch if not lazy-loaded)

---

### 6. EQUATION & COMPUTED COLUMNS

**Price equation system:** `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts`
- **Triggered by:** margin/sell price/royalty cell edits via valueSetter (lines 1781-1983 for Gen/Lic columns)

```typescript
}
```
- Trivial: just persists filter state to sessionStorage
- No side-effects on grid behavior

9. **Carton dimensions, landed cost, equations:** Either pre-compute on server or trigger client recalc after edit
10. **Undo/redo:** May break if row data is refreshed from server mid-undo-chain; consider disabling or clearing undo on server sync

---

- `rfq.grid.config.ts`: Remove client-side computed columns or mark as server-cached
- Backend API: Implement paginated getRows endpoint with filters, sorting, division params
- `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`: Ensure Vendors array is available (may need separate fetch)

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

---

## FINDINGS: RFQ List Query Analysis

### 1. Customer.getAll Function (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\models\lib.model.js, lines 347-482)

```javascript
Customer.getAll = async (req, result) => {
  let token = req.headers.authorization;
  let user = await getUserInfo(token)
  let status;
  let condition;
  let order;
  let divisionCodeArray = ['Lic,Gen', 'Gen,Lic'];
  let divisionStatusArray = [];
  let subQuery = false;
  
  // Reads req.body fields:
  if (req.body.status == 'archive') {
    status = 1
  } else {
    status = null
  }
  for (var i = 0; req.body.divisionCode.length > i; i++) {
    divisionCodeArray.push(req.body.divisionCode[i].divCode_code)
  }
  for (var i = 0; req.body.divisionStatus.length > i; i++) {
    divisionStatusArray.push(req.body.divisionStatus[i].RFQItemStatus_id)
  }
  
  // VENDOR ROLE CONDITIONS (lines 367-428)
  if (user.role == 'vendor') {
    // ... vendor filter logic ...
    if (req.body.filterd_rfq == 'true') {
      // Allow showing highlighted item OR items assigned to vendor/factory
      condition = {
        '$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$': vendorArchived,
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
        [Op.or]: [
          { '$RFQVendors.vendor_id_fk$': vendorFilter },
          { 'rfqItem_style_number': req.body.highlightedItem }
        ]
      }
    } else {
      condition = {
        '$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$': vendorArchived,
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
        '$RFQVendors.vendor_id_fk$': vendorFilter
      }
    }
    order = [Sequelize.literal('"RFQVendor_id" DESC')]
  } else {
    // INTERNAL ROLE CONDITIONS (lines 432-446)
    condition = {
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_archive$': status,
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
    }
    order = [Sequelize.literal('"rfqItem_id" DESC')]
    subQuery = true
  }

  const rfqVendorsInclude = buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user);

  try {
    const [data, merchGroupData, whsecost] = await Promise.all([
      sql.RFQItem.findAll({
        order: order,
        subQuery: subQuery,
        attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES },
        as: 'RFQItem',
        where: condition,
        include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, rfqVendorsInclude),
      }),
      getCachedMerchGroup(sql),
      getCachedWhsePrice(sql),
    ]);

    // ... remap and sort ...
    items = items.sort(function (a, b) {
      return (a['Price Code'] == req.body.highlightedItem) ? -1 : 1;
    });

    result(null, items);
  } catch (err) {
    result(err, null);
  }
};
```

**Request body fields consumed:**
- `req.body.status` — 'archive' or null
- `req.body.divisionCode` — array of {divCode_code}
- `req.body.divisionStatus` — array of {RFQItemStatus_id}
- `req.body.filterd_rfq` — 'true' or null (vendor-only, filtered mode)
- `req.body.highlightedItem` — rfqItem_style_number to bump to top of sort

**Note:** No pagination (`limit`/`offset`) or sorting/filtering params are currently read or applied in SQL.

---

### 2. Include Builders and Eager Loads

#### buildRfqListLookupIncludes (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 109-159)

```javascript
function buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, rfqVendorsInclude) {
  return [
    rfqVendorsInclude,  // ← RFQVendor + nested Factory
    {
      model: sql.customers,
      as: 'customer',
      attributes: ['customers_id', 'customers_name', 'customers_code', 'customers_dilution', 'customers_logistic_load'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQItemStatus,
      as: 'RFQItemStatus',
      attributes: ['RFQItemStatus_id', 'RFQItemStatus_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.itemDepth,
      as: 'itemDepth',
      attributes: ['itemDepth_id', 'itemDepth_code', 'itemDepth_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.deliveryLocation,
      as: 'deliveryLocation',
      attributes: ['deliveryLocation_id', 'deliveryLocation_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQGroup,
      as: 'RFQGroup',
      attributes: ['RFQGroup_id', 'RFQGroup_name'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQStep,
      as: 'RFQStep',
      attributes: ['RFQStep_id', 'RFQStep_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.Factory,
      as: 'Factory',
      attributes: FACTORY_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,  // [id, factory_name, factory_nickname, factory_status, factory_access, factory_country, sort_order]
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQContainer,
      as: 'RFQContainer',
      attributes: ['RFQContainer_id', 'RFQContainer_price'],
    },
  ];
}
```

#### buildRfqVendorsInclude (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 161-183)

```javascript
function buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user) {
  const include = {
    model: sql.RFQVendor,
    as: 'RFQVendors',
    required: false,
    attributes: RFQ_VENDOR_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,  // [RFQVendor_id, RFQVendor_amount, RFQVendor_cbm_pc, RFQVendor_status, RFQitem_id_fk, RFQVendor_note, carton_*, req_status, vendor_id_fk, quote_date, std_vendor_id_fk, lead_time, price_terms, fob_country, fob_port, requote_requested, RFQVendor_archived, RFQVendor_archive_optout]
    include: [{
      model: sql.Factory,
      as: 'Factory',
      required: false,
      attributes: FACTORY_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,
    }],
  };
  if (user.role === 'vendor') {
    include.where = { vendor_id_fk: vendorFilterIds... };
  }
  return include;
}
```

**Eager-loaded associations:**
- `RFQVendors` (left join, optional) → RFQVendor with nested Factory
- `customer` (left join)
- `RFQItemStatus` (left join)
- `itemDepth` (left join)
- `deliveryLocation` (left join)
- `RFQGroup` (left join)
- `RFQStep` (left join)
- `Factory` (left join) — "Chosen Vendor"
- `RFQContainer` (left join)

**Critical:** `subQuery: true` for internal roles (line 445). With 8 includes and a left-join-heavy structure, subQuery is necessary for LIMIT to work correctly (prevents row multiplication).

```javascript
  }
  for (var i = 0; req.body.divisionStatus.length > i; i++) {
    divisionStatusArray.push(req.body.divisionStatus[i].RFQItemStatus_id)
  }
  
  // VENDOR ROLE CONDITIONS (lines 367-428)
  if (user.role == 'vendor') {
    // ... vendor filter logic ...
    if (req.body.filterd_rfq == 'true') {
      // Allow showing highlighted item OR items assigned to vendor/factory
      condition = {
        '$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$': vendorArchived,
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
        [Op.or]: [
          { '$RFQVendors.vendor_id_fk$': vendorFilter },
          { 'rfqItem_style_number': req.body.highlightedItem }
        ]
      }
    } else {
      condition = {
        '$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$': vendorArchived,
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
        '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
        '$RFQVendors.vendor_id_fk$': vendorFilter
      }
    }
    order = [Sequelize.literal('"RFQVendor_id" DESC')]
  } else {
    // INTERNAL ROLE CONDITIONS (lines 432-446)
    condition = {
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_archive$': status,
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_divCode_id_fk$': { [Op.or]: divisionCodeArray },
      '$RFQItem.rfqItem_active$': { [Op.or]: divisionStatusArray },
    }
    order = [Sequelize.literal('"rfqItem_id" DESC')]
    subQuery = true
  }

  const rfqVendorsInclude = buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user);

  try {
    const [data, merchGroupData, whsecost] = await Promise.all([
      sql.RFQItem.findAll({
        order: order,
        subQuery: subQuery,
        attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES },
        as: 'RFQItem',
        where: condition,
        include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, rfqVendorsInclude),
      }),
      getCachedMerchGroup(sql),
      getCachedWhsePrice(sql),
    ]);

    // ... remap and sort ...
    items = items.sort(function (a, b) {
      return (a['Price Code'] == req.body.highlightedItem) ? -1 : 1;
    });

    result(null, items);
  } catch (err) {
    result(err, null);
  }
};
```

**Request body fields consumed:**
- `req.body.status` — 'archive' or null
- `req.body.divisionCode` — array of {divCode_code}
- `req.body.divisionStatus` — array of {RFQItemStatus_id}
- `req.body.filterd_rfq` — 'true' or null (vendor-only, filtered mode)
- `req.body.highlightedItem` — rfqItem_style_number to bump to top of sort

**Note:** No pagination (`limit`/`offset`) or sorting/filtering params are currently read or applied in SQL.

---

### 2. Include Builders and Eager Loads

#### buildRfqListLookupIncludes (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 109-159)

```javascript
function buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, rfqVendorsInclude) {
  return [
    rfqVendorsInclude,  // ← RFQVendor + nested Factory
    {
      model: sql.customers,
      as: 'customer',
      attributes: ['customers_id', 'customers_name', 'customers_code', 'customers_dilution', 'customers_logistic_load'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQItemStatus,
      as: 'RFQItemStatus',
      attributes: ['RFQItemStatus_id', 'RFQItemStatus_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.itemDepth,
      as: 'itemDepth',
      attributes: ['itemDepth_id', 'itemDepth_code', 'itemDepth_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.deliveryLocation,
      as: 'deliveryLocation',
      attributes: ['deliveryLocation_id', 'deliveryLocation_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQGroup,
      as: 'RFQGroup',
      attributes: ['RFQGroup_id', 'RFQGroup_name'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQStep,
      as: 'RFQStep',
      attributes: ['RFQStep_id', 'RFQStep_title'],
    },
    {
      model: sql.Factory,
      as: 'Factory',
      attributes: FACTORY_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,  // [id, factory_name, factory_nickname, factory_status, factory_access, factory_country, sort_order]
    },
    {
      model: sql.RFQContainer,
      as: 'RFQContainer',
      attributes: ['RFQContainer_id', 'RFQContainer_price'],
    },
  ];
}
```

#### buildRfqVendorsInclude (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 161-183)

```javascript
function buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user) {
  const include = {
    model: sql.RFQVendor,
    as: 'RFQVendors',
    required: false,
    attributes: RFQ_VENDOR_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,  // [RFQVendor_id, RFQVendor_amount, RFQVendor_cbm_pc, RFQVendor_status, RFQitem_id_fk, RFQVendor_note, carton_*, req_status, vendor_id_fk, quote_date, std_vendor_id_fk, lead_time, price_terms, fob_country, fob_port, requote_requested, RFQVendor_archived, RFQVendor_archive_optout]
    include: [{
      model: sql.Factory,
      as: 'Factory',
      required: false,
      attributes: FACTORY_LIST_ATTRIBUTES,
    }],
  };
  if (user.role === 'vendor') {
    include.where = { vendor_id_fk: vendorFilterIds... };
  }
  return include;
}
```

**Eager-loaded associations:**
- `RFQVendors` (left join, optional) → RFQVendor with nested Factory
- `customer` (left join)
- `RFQItemStatus` (left join)
- `itemDepth` (left join)
- `deliveryLocation` (left join)
- `RFQGroup` (left join)
- `RFQStep` (left join)
- `Factory` (left join) — "Chosen Vendor"
- `RFQContainer` (left join)

**Critical:** `subQuery: true` for internal roles (line 445). With 8 includes and a left-join-heavy structure, subQuery is necessary for LIMIT to work correctly (prevents row multiplication).

#### RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js, lines 5-17)

```javascript
const RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES = [
  'rfqItem_auditlog',
  'rfqItem_default_cbm',
  'rfqItem_gen_fob_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_whse_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_poe_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_mddp_netsell',
  'rfqItem_gen_ldp_margin',
  'rfqItem_cbm_per_piece',
  'rfqItem_quote_update',
  'rfqItem_price_per_cbm',
  'rfqItem_standardized_products',
];
```

**COMPUTED IN JAVASCRIPT (NO DB COLUMN):**

**Numeric parse + assignment side effects:**
- 'FOB Cost' (line 78-85) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_fob_cost)`, stores in global `fobCost`
- 'Case Pack' (line 88-95) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_case_pack)`, stores in global `casePack`

- 'Duty Rate %' (line 96-102) — transforms to object `{ title: parseFloat(), duty_rate_dollar_amount: parseFloat() }`
- 'Logistics Load %' (line 132-138) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_logistic_load)`, stores in global `logisticsLoad`
- 'royalty' (line 59-65) — `parseFloat(rfqItem_royalty)`, stores in global `royalty`

**Object reconstruction from includes (PERSISTED, but assembled from relations):**
- 'Group' (line 39) ← `rfqItem_udf1` passed to `resolveMerchGroupField()` → object `{ title: mg_desc, mg_code, id }`
- 'Material' (line 40) ← `rfqItem_udf2` → merch group object
- 'Treatment' (line 41) ← `rfqItem_udf3` → merch group object
- 'Size (LxW)' (line 42) ← `rfqItem_size_l_w` → merch group object
- 'Construction' (line 43) ← `rfqItem_udf4` → merch group object
- 'Depth' (line 52) — from `itemDepth` include, assembled into `{ title, id, code }`
- 'Step' (line 53-57) — from `RFQStep` include, object with `title`, `id`, `factoriesStepUpdatedAt`
- 'Customer' (line 70) — from `customer` include, object with `title`, `id`, `code`, `dilution`, `logistics_load`
- 'RFQ Group' (line 105) — from `RFQGroup` include, object with `RFQGroup_name`, `RFQGroup_id`
- 'Status' (line 109) — from `RFQItemStatus` include, object with `title`, `id`
- 'Chosen Vendor' (line 112) — from `Factory` include, object with `title: factory_nickname`, `id`
- 'Delivery Location' (line 124) — from `deliveryLocation` include, object with `title`, `id`

**HIGHLY COMPUTED (complex logic, reference data merges):**
- 'Res' (line 150-169) — counts unique responded vs requested vendors from RFQVendors array: `{unique_responded.size}/{unique_requested.size}`. Iterates all RFQVendor rows, filters by `req_status !== 1`, checks if `RFQVendor_amount` is non-NaN. **Cannot be sorted/filtered in SQL without replicating this logic in a computed column or subquery.**
- 'Vendors' (line 211-256) — transforms full RFQVendors array into detail list of vendor objects. Sorts by price. Maps each to include Factory nickname, quote_date, lead_time, etc. **Cannot be sorted/filtered in SQL; entirely JS-side.**
- 'containercost' (line 37) — from `RFQContainer` include, transforms to object if present

**Reference data transforms (requires IN-MEMORY LOOKUP):**
- 'requested_price_cells' (lines 262-267) — `JSON.parse(rfqItem_requested_price_cells)` with fallback to `[]`
- 'price_sales_snapshots' (lines 268-273) — `JSON.parse(rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots)` with fallback to `{}`

### 5. Routing: /getAllItem to Customer.getAll

BFF proxy (`designflow-bff/src/backendProxy.js`) forwards `/api/core/*` to `CORE_BACKEND_URL` (designflow-backend) with OIDC identity token.

---

### 6. Pagination/Sort/Filter Translation Helpers

**None exist currently.** The codebase has:
- **Line 445 in lib.model.js:** `subQuery = true` for internal roles only (Sequelize workaround for LIMIT with joins)
- **Line 415, 427, 444:** `Sequelize.literal()` raw SQL order clauses
- **No offset/limit:** All rows returned in memory, then sorted by JS (line 474-476)

**Potential reuse patterns found:**
- `rfqListQuery.js` helper already encapsulates `buildRfqListLookupIncludes()`, `buildRfqVendorsInclude()`, and cache functions
- Test suite (`tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`) demonstrates builder patterns

---

## SUMMARY TABLE: Computed vs DB-Backed (Key Grid Columns)

| Column | DB Column | Computed in JS | Sortable in SQL | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price Code | rfqItem_style_number | No | YES | Direct string |
| Description | rfqItem_description | No | YES | Direct string |
| Status | rfqItem_active (ID) | Partial | YES (ID only) | ID stored in DB, title from join |
| Date Modified | rfqItem_date_modified | No | YES | Direct DATE |
| Created Date | rfqItem_created_date | No | YES | Direct DATE |
| RFQ Group | rfqItem_rfq_group | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, name from RFQGroup join |
| Customer | rfqItem_customer | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, details from customer join |
| FOB Cost | rfqItem_fob_cost | Partial | YES (requires CAST) | Stored as STRING, needs numeric cast to sort |
| Landed Cost | rfqItem_landed_cost | No | YES | Stored as STRING, persisted |
| CBM/pc | rfqItem_cbm_per_price | No | YES | Stored as STRING |
| Factory (Chosen Vendor) | rfqItem_choosen_vendor | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, name from Factory join |
| **Res (responded/requested)** | **NONE** | **YES, fully** | **NO** | Iterates RFQVendors, counts non-null prices vs req_status. Must compute post-fetch. |
| **Group** | rfqItem_udf1 | Yes | INDIRECT | Merch group lookup. Stored as merch group ID, but display name requires in-memory map lookup. |
| **Material** | rfqItem_udf2 | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group. |
| **Treatment** | rfqItem_udf3 | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group. |
| **Construction** | rfqItem_udf4 | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group. |
| **Size (LxW)** | rfqItem_size_l_w | Yes | INDIRECT | Same as Group — merch group lookup. |
| Depth | rfqItem_delivery_loc | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, title from itemDepth join |
| Step | rfqItem_step | No | YES (with JOIN) | ID in DB, title from RFQStep join |

content.text:
Exploration is thorough. I now understand the full picture, and it surfaced **two decisions that fundamentally shape the implementation** — I need your call before I finalize the plan.

The big one: **the RFQ grid is grouped by "RFQ Group" by default** (collapsible groups, `rowGroup: true`, recency-ordered via `postSortRows`). The other SSRM grids (prod_tracking, itemLibrary) are *flat* lists. Server-side row grouping in AG Grid is a much larger, riskier backend effort (the server must return group rows, handle expansion, aggregate, and order groups by recency) — whereas flattening to a paginated list matches the proven pattern and makes sort/filter-on-all-rows straightforward.

Second: a few columns (**"Res" responded/requested counts, carton dimensions, and merch-group display-name filtering**) are computed in JS with no DB column, so they can't be server-sorted/filtered without extra SQL work.

questions.question: The RFQ grid currently groups rows by "RFQ Group" (collapsible, recency-ordered). SSRM changes how grouping works. Which do you want?

options.label: Flatten to a paginated list
options.description: Drop the default collapsible RFQ-Group grouping; show a flat, paginated, recency-sorted list. 'RFQ Group' stays a normal sortable/filterable column. Matches the proven prod_tracking/itemLibrary SSRM pattern — simplest and lowest-risk, and makes sort/filter-on-all-rows clean.

options.label: Keep server-side grouping
options.description: Preserve the collapsible grouped-by-RFQ-Group view under SSRM. Much larger/riskier backend: server must return group rows, handle expand-to-load children, aggregate, and replicate the cross-group recency ordering. Significantly more code + harder to verify.

questions.question: A few columns are computed in JS (no DB column): "Res" (responded/requested count), carton dimensions, and merch-group filtering by display name. How should server-side sort/filter handle them?

options.label: Disable sort/filter on those few
options.description: Everything DB-backed (Price Code, Description, dates, prices, Status, Customer, Chosen Vendor, Step, etc.) gets full server-side sort/filter across ALL rows. The handful of purely-computed columns (Res, carton dims, merch-group-by-name) are marked non-sortable/non-filterable. Pragmatic and correct.

options.label: Replicate them in SQL too
options.description: Add SQL subqueries/joins so Res (vendor counts), carton dims (from vendor), and merch-group names are ALSO server-sortable/filterable. Broader coverage, but notably more backend code and risk.

questions.question: Export currently dumps the loaded rows. Under SSRM the client only holds the fetched page(s). What should Export do?

options.label: Full filtered set (server-side)
options.description: Add an export path that fetches ALL rows matching the current filters/sort from the backend, so Export reflects the whole dataset, not just loaded pages. Correct behavior; a bit more backend work.

options.label: Loaded pages only
options.description: Keep export client-side — only exports rows currently cached in the grid. Simplest, but exports partial data and can silently omit rows. Not recommended.

content.content: Your questions have been answered: "The RFQ grid currently groups rows by "RFQ Group" (collapsible, recency-ordered). SSRM changes how grouping works. Which do you want?"="Keep server-side grouping", "A few columns are computed in JS (no DB column): "Res" (responded/requested count), carton dimensions, and merch-group filtering by display name. How should server-side sort/filter handle them?"="i don't understand the difference and the full effects of these 2 choices", "Export currently dumps the loaded rows. Under SSRM the client only holds the fetched page(s). What should Export do?"="Full filtered set (server-side)". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

options.description: Preserve the collapsible grouped-by-RFQ-Group view under SSRM. Much larger/riskier backend: server must return group rows, handle expand-to-load children, aggregate, and replicate the cross-group recency ordering. Significantly more code + harder to verify.
