options.label: Disable sort/filter on those few
options.description: Everything DB-backed (Price Code, Description, dates, prices, Status, Customer, Chosen Vendor, Step, etc.) gets full server-side sort/filter across ALL rows. The handful of purely-computed columns (Res, carton dims, merch-group-by-name) are marked non-sortable/non-filterable. Pragmatic and correct.

options.label: Replicate them in SQL too
options.description: Add SQL subqueries/joins so Res (vendor counts), carton dims (from vendor), and merch-group names are ALSO server-sortable/filterable. Broader coverage, but notably more backend code and risk.

questions.question: Export currently dumps the loaded rows. Under SSRM the client only holds the fetched page(s). What should Export do?

options.label: Full filtered set (server-side)
options.description: Add an export path that fetches ALL rows matching the current filters/sort from the backend, so Export reflects the whole dataset, not just loaded pages. Correct behavior; a bit more backend work.

options.label: Loaded pages only
options.description: Keep export client-side — only exports rows currently cached in the grid. Simplest, but exports partial data and can silently omit rows. Not recommended.

content.text:
Good — server-side grouping and full server-side export it is. Let me explain the computed-columns choice plainly, because it changes how much backend work there is.

**What "sort/filter" means under SSRM:** when you click a column header to sort, or type in a column's filter box, the grid asks the *server* "give me the rows, sorted/filtered by this column." The server answers with SQL. SQL can only sort or filter on something that exists **in the database**.

**Most columns are real DB columns** — Price Code, Description, all the prices, dates, Status, Customer, Chosen Vendor, Step, RFQ Group, etc. Those will sort/filter across all rows with no problem.

**Three columns are *not* stored** — they're calculated on the fly:
- **"Res"** (the "3/5" responded-count) — computed by counting vendor rows; there's no `Res` column in the DB.
- **Carton length/width/height** — read from the *chosen vendor's* row, not stored on the RFQ item.
- **Merch-group columns by name** (Group / Material / Treatment / Product-Type / Size) — the DB stores an *ID*; the human name ("Cotton") comes from a lookup loaded in memory. The filter box shows names, which the DB doesn't have directly.

The two choices, and their real effects:

- **Disable sort/filter on those three** — they still *display* perfectly; you just can't click-to-sort or type-to-filter on them. **Every other column** sorts/filters across all rows. Less code, lower risk.
- **Replicate them in SQL** — I add a counting subquery (Res), a join to the chosen vendor (carton dims), and joins to the merch-group tables (names) so those columns *also* sort/filter server-side. Full coverage, but meaningfully more backend code and more chance of bugs/slowness — on top of the server-side grouping you already chose, which is itself the complex path.

My recommendation is to **disable** on those three (you keep ~95% of columns fully working and keep risk contained), but it's your call:

questions.question: For the 3 computed columns (Res, carton dimensions, merch-group-by-name) — which can't be sort/filtered in SQL without extra work — what do you want? (All other columns sort/filter across all rows either way.)

options.label: Disable sort/filter on those 3 (Recommended)
options.description: Res, carton dims, and the merch-group columns still display normally, but their column header sort and filter box are turned off. Everything else (Price Code, Description, prices, dates, Status, Customer, Chosen Vendor, Step, RFQ Group, etc.) sorts and filters across ALL rows. Least code, lowest risk on top of the server-side grouping you chose.

options.label: Replicate all 3 in SQL
options.description: I add a vendor-counting subquery (Res), a chosen-vendor join (carton dims), and merch-group table joins (names) so those columns ALSO sort/filter server-side. Every column becomes sortable/filterable, but it's notably more backend code, more query complexity, and more risk/slowness — stacked on the server-side grouping work.

That's the complex path on every axis, so let me pressure-test the riskiest piece — the backend SSRM-with-grouping query layer — with a focused Plan agent before I write the final plan.

input.prompt:
Design (do NOT write code) the backend query layer for migrating the RFQ list in C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend to AG Grid Server-Side Row Model (SSRM) WITH server-side row grouping. Output a concrete implementation design with Sequelize specifics and file/function targets.

CONTEXT (already explored):
- Current endpoint: POST /getAllItem → controllers/main.controller.js `findAll` → `Customer.getAll` in models/lib.model.js (~line 347-482). It does an unbounded `sql.RFQItem.findAll` with `subQuery:true` (internal role), `attributes:{exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES}`, `where: condition`, `include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(...)`, then `utility.remapRFQItem(data, merchGroupData, {merchGroupMap, whsecost})` and returns the full array.
- Query helpers live in C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js: `buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql,user)`, `buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, rfqVendorsInclude)`, `RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES`, `getCachedMerchGroup(sql)`, `getCachedWhsePrice(sql)`. Includes: RFQVendors(+Factory), customer, RFQItemStatus, itemDepth, deliveryLocation, RFQGroup, RFQStep, Factory, RFQContainer.
- remapRFQItem (helpers/utility.js) builds client rows. DB-backed columns map to: 'Price Code'=rfqItem_style_number, 'Description'=rfqItem_description, 'Date Modified'=rfqItem_date_modified (DATE), 'Created Date'=rfqItem_created_date, 'FOB Cost'=rfqItem_fob_cost (STRING), 'Landed Cost $'=rfqItem_landed_cost (STRING), 'CBM/pc'=rfqItem_cbm_per_price (STRING), 'Quantity'=rfqItem_quantity (STRING), 'Status'=rfqItem_active (id)+RFQItemStatus join, 'RFQ Group'=rfqItem_rfq_group→RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name, 'Customer'=rfqItem_customer→customers join, 'Chosen Vendor'=rfqItem_choosen_vendor→Factory join, 'Step'=rfqItem_step→RFQStep join, 'Depth'→itemDepth, 'Delivery Location'→deliveryLocation, many gen/lic margin/price/royalty are direct rfqItem_* columns.
- Merch-group columns Group/Material/Treatment/Construction/Size are stored as IDs in rfqItem_udf1..udf4 and rfqItem_size_l_w; display names come from getCachedMerchGroup (in-memory map id→{mg_desc,mg_code}). 'Res' (responded/requested e.g. "3/5") is computed in JS from the RFQVendors array (responded = RFQVendor with non-null numeric RFQVendor_amount; requested = vendors with req_status != 1 logic) — NO db column. 'Vendors' detail array is built from RFQVendors (needed for master/detail). Carton dims come from the chosen vendor.
- Existing where `condition` for internal role: rfqItem_archive (status null/1), rfqItem_divCode_id_fk IN divisionCodeArray, rfqItem_active IN divisionStatusArray. Vendor role has its own condition (RFQVendors.vendor_id_fk filter, RFQVendor_archived). highlightedItem bumps a row to top.

REQUIREMENTS (user decisions):
1. SSRM WITH server-side ROW GROUPING by 'RFQ Group', groups ordered by most-recent child 'Date Modified' (descending), matching the current postSortRows behavior.
2. Server-side FILTERING and SORTING must work across ALL rows (not just a page), for all DB-backed columns. Filter types in use: agTextColumnFilter, agMultiColumnFilter (normalize to first sub-filter), agSetColumnFilter (Step, Status etc.), agDateColumnFilter (Date Modified, Factory_updated). Numeric-but-stored-as-string columns (FOB Cost, Landed Cost, CBM/pc, Quantity) must sort/filter numerically (CAST).
3. 'Res' MUST be filterable AND sortable — design correlated subqueries on RFQVendor to compute responded & requested counts, expose Res as a value the AG text filter can match (the user sees "responded/requested"), and allow WHERE/ORDER on it.
4. Merch-group columns (Group/Material/Treatment/Construction/Size) MUST be filterable. Prefer translating filter values (names) → merch-group IDs via the cached merch-group map, then filtering on rfqItem_udf*/size_l_w IN (ids) — avoid heavy joins if possible. Discuss feasibility for both set filters (values[]) and text filters (contains).
5. Carton dimensions do NOT need sort/filter.
6. Master/detail: each returned LEAF row must still include the 'Vendors' array (remapRFQItem already builds it) so the detail grid keeps working.
7. Full-dataset server-side EXPORT: an endpoint that returns ALL rows matching the current filters/sort (no pagination), reusing the same filter/sort translation.
8. Preserve vendor-role behavior, division filters, archive flag, and highlightedItem.

Deliver a precise design covering:
A. The AG Grid SSRM request contract for grouping: how to read request.rowGroupCols, request.groupKeys, request.sortModel, request.filterModel, startRow, endRow. Exactly when to return GROUP rows vs LEAF rows (groupKeys.length vs rowGroupCols.length), and the response shape {rows, lastRow/totalCount} for each level. How AG Grid identifies group rows (the group key field) and how getRowId/getServerSideGroupKey should behave for groups vs leaves.
B. The SQL for the GROUP level: SELECT distinct RFQGroup with the recency ordering (max child date_modified) and pagination, applying the same filter (so groups with no matching children disappear). Sequelize approach (group, attributes with fn/col/literal, having, order, limit/offset, count of distinct groups for lastRow).
C. The SQL for the LEAF level: the existing findAll + includes, plus where RFQ Group = groupKeys[0], plus translated filter/sort, plus limit/offset, plus a distinct count for lastRow. How to keep subQuery/distinct correct so LIMIT applies to RFQItem rows not joined rows.
D. A reusable field→DB-column map and two translators: filterModel→Sequelize where (text/set/date/number, multi normalization, numeric CAST), and sortModel→Sequelize order. Where to put them (extend helpers/rfqListQuery.js). How to handle lookup columns (Status/Customer/Step/Chosen Vendor/Depth/Delivery Location) — filter/sort by joined name vs FK id, and the subQuery implications.
E. Res subquery design (responded & requested counts) usable in SELECT, WHERE, and ORDER.
F. Merch-group filter translation design.
G. New/changed files & functions (controller, route, model, helpers), and how to keep the OLD non-paginated path working during rollout (e.g. new endpoints alongside getAll, or a flag).
H. Risks, Sequelize gotchas (subQuery + group + include + limit interactions; ordering by aggregates; filtering on correlated subqueries in WHERE), and a test plan (unit tests for the translators + group/leaf query builders, mirroring tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js).

Be concrete and reference real file paths/functions. Return the design only.

I don't have a Write tool available (read-only planning mode, and Write isn't in the deferred list). I'll deliver the design directly as my response, which is the correct output for this planning task.

---

# Design: Backend Query Layer for AG Grid SSRM with Server-Side Row Grouping (RFQ List)

This design adds a paginated, server-side-grouped, server-filtered/sorted query layer for the RFQ list while leaving the existing `POST /getAllItem` → `Customer.getAll` path fully intact for rollback. All new query logic lands in `helpers/rfqListQuery.js`; a thin model method and controller/route are added.

Confirmed facts grounding this design:
- Recency-ordered group query pattern already exists at `models/lib.model.js:1970-1986` (`SELECT MAX("rfqItem_date_modified") FROM "RFQItem" WHERE "rfqItem_rfq_group" = "RFQGroup"."RFQGroup_id"`, with `Sequelize.literal('"last_activity" DESC NULLS LAST')` and the schema-prefix idiom `process.env.schema ? '"'+schema+'".' : ''`).
- Tables: `RFQItem` (PK `rfqItem_id`, group FK `rfqItem_rfq_group` → `RFQGroup.RFQGroup_id`), `RFQVendor` (PK `RFQVendor_id`, FK `RFQitem_id_fk`, amount `RFQVendor_amount` STRING, `req_status`, `vendor_id_fk`).
- Aliases used by `buildRfqListLookupIncludes` are real (configured via UDFTable dynamic associations): `RFQVendors`, `customer`, `RFQItemStatus`, `itemDepth`, `deliveryLocation`, `RFQGroup`, `RFQStep`, `Factory`, `RFQContainer`.
- `remapRFQItem` (`helpers/utility.js:19-`) already builds the `Vendors` array and the `Res` string per row; it mutates `item.dataValues` in place, so it can be reused unchanged on leaf rows.

---

## A. AG Grid SSRM request contract (grouping)

The frontend datasource POSTs the standard `IServerSideGetRowsRequest`. The controller reads:

- `rowGroupCols`: array of grouped columns. For this feature it is always `[{ id, displayName, field }]` for the **RFQ Group** column (the UI is configured with `rowGroup: true` on that one column).
- `groupKeys`: the expanded group path. `[]` = top level (return group rows). `["GroupName"]` = inside one group (return that group's leaf rows).
- `sortModel`: `[{ colId, sort: 'asc'|'desc' }, ...]`.
- `filterModel`: `{ [colId]: <filter def> }`.
- `startRow`, `endRow`: pagination window (compute `limit = endRow - startRow`, `offset = startRow`).
- Plus our existing body fields preserved verbatim: `divisionCode`, `divisionStatus`, `status` ('archive' or not), `filterd_rfq`, `highlightedItem`. Auth header → `getUserInfo(token)` as today.

**Level decision rule** (mirrors AG Grid's own logic):

```
const groupingActive = rowGroupCols.length > 0;
const atLeafLevel    = !groupingActive || groupKeys.length >= rowGroupCols.length;
```

- `groupingActive && groupKeys.length < rowGroupCols.length` → **GROUP level** (Section B). Here `groupKeys.length === 0` and we return one row per matching RFQ Group.
- `atLeafLevel` → **LEAF level** (Section C). The active group is `groupKeys[0]` (the RFQGroup_name).

**Response shape (both levels):**

```json
{ "rows": [...], "lastRow": <number|-1> }
```

- `lastRow` is the total count at the current level (groups total for group level; leaf total within the group for leaf level). Return it whenever known so AG Grid stops requesting blocks; return `-1` when the returned block is full and we did not compute a count (we will always compute it via `COUNT(DISTINCT ...)`, so `lastRow` is always a real number here).
- Group rows must carry the **group key field**. Because the grid groups on the `RFQ Group` column whose value is an object `{ RFQGroup_name, RFQGroup_id }`, the cleanest contract is to make the SSRM **group key be the string `RFQGroup_name`**. Two supported approaches:
  - Configure the column's `keyCreator` / use `getServerSideGroupKey` so the group key equals `RFQGroup_name` (a primitive string). Group rows then only need `{ "RFQ Group": "<name>" }` (plus optional aggregated display fields like child count / last activity). `groupKeys[0]` arriving on the next request will be exactly that string.
  - `getRowId`: for **leaf** rows return the stable `id` (`rfqItem_id`, already emitted by `remapRFQItem` as `id`). For **group** rows, AG Grid manages group node ids from the group key; do not supply leaf `getRowId` logic for group-level rows (guard with `params.data?.id != null`).

So the server contract is: group rows = `{ "RFQ Group": name, childCount, lastActivity }`; leaf rows = full `remapRFQItem` objects (which include `id` and `Vendors`).

---

## B. GROUP-level SQL

Goal: distinct RFQ Groups that have ≥1 child matching the **same leaf filter**, ordered by most-recent child `rfqItem_date_modified` desc, paginated, plus a total distinct-group count for `lastRow`.

Do **not** use `RFQGroup.findAll` here (the existing `getRFQGroup` lists all groups regardless of children). Instead aggregate from `RFQItem` so empty/non-matching groups disappear and recency comes for free:

```js
// helpers/rfqListQuery.js  → buildGroupLevelQuery(sql, { user, baseCondition, filterModel, schemaPrefix })
const where = { [Op.and]: [ baseCondition, translateFilterModel(filterModel, /*excludeGroupCol*/ true) ] };

const queryOptions = {
  attributes: [
    [Sequelize.col('RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name'), 'rfqGroupName'],
    [Sequelize.col('RFQGroup.RFQGroup_id'),   'rfqGroupId'],
    [Sequelize.fn('MAX', Sequelize.col('RFQItem.rfqItem_date_modified')), 'lastActivity'],
    [Sequelize.fn('COUNT', Sequelize.col('RFQItem.rfqItem_id')), 'childCount'],
  ],
  include: [ /* only includes referenced by the active filter (see note) */ ],
  where,
  group: ['RFQGroup.RFQGroup_id', 'RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name'],
  order: [[Sequelize.literal('"lastActivity" DESC NULLS LAST')]],
  limit, offset,
  subQuery: false,
  raw: true,
  nest: false,
};
```

Notes:
- The `RFQGroup` include must be `required: true` so groups are joinable; FK is `rfqItem_rfq_group`.
- **Filter must apply to the same population.** Translate `filterModel` to a Sequelize `where` (Section D) and AND it with `baseCondition` (division/archive/active/vendor). Because a group with zero matching children produces no rows in the grouped result, "groups with no matching children disappear" is automatic.
- **Res / numeric-string filters that need correlated subqueries** (Section E/D) are added as `where: Sequelize.literal('(<corr subquery>) ...')` clauses; they correlate on `RFQItem.rfqItem_id` and work inside the grouped query.
- **lastRow / total group count:** a `GROUP BY` with `findAndCountAll` returns per-group counts, not a scalar — do **not** rely on it. Instead run a parallel `COUNT(DISTINCT rfqItem_rfq_group)` over the same `where`:

```js
const totalGroups = await sql.RFQItem.count({
  where, include: groupFilterIncludes,
  distinct: true, col: 'rfqItem_rfq_group',
});
```

Run the page query and the count concurrently (`Promise.all`), plus `getCachedMerchGroup`/`getCachedWhsePrice` only if merch-group filters are present.
- Map each row to `{ "RFQ Group": row.rfqGroupName, childCount: row.childCount, lastActivity: row.lastActivity }`. Return `{ rows, lastRow: totalGroups }`.
- **highlightedItem:** at group level, if a highlighted style number exists, optionally bump its group to top after ordering (find which group contains it via a cheap lookup). Simpler: keep recency order at group level and only bump the leaf row inside its group (Section C). Recommend the latter to preserve recency semantics — matches current behavior closest since `postSortRows` is the dominant rule.

---

## C. LEAF-level SQL

Reuse the existing findAll shape (`attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES }`, `include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(...)`), then add: group filter, translated filter/sort, pagination, and a correct distinct count.

```js
// buildLeafLevelQuery(sql, { user, baseCondition, groupName, filterModel, sortModel, schemaPrefix })
const where = {
  [Op.and]: [
    baseCondition,
    { '$RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name$': groupName },   // groupKeys[0]
    translateFilterModel(filterModel, /*excludeGroupCol*/ true),
  ],
};

const findOptions = {
  attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES },
  where,
  include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user)),
  order: translateSortModel(sortModel, { schemaPrefix }),  // + tiebreak rfqItem_id DESC
  limit, offset,
  subQuery: true,     // critical: see gotcha
  distinct: true,     // critical for count correctness
};
```

**subQuery / distinct correctness (the central pitfall):**
- `RFQVendors` (and the vendor-role `where` on it) is a `hasMany` include. With `limit` + a `hasMany` join, Postgres would multiply rows and `LIMIT` would cut joined rows, not RFQItems. Sequelize's fix is `subQuery: true`, which wraps the primary table in a subquery so `LIMIT/OFFSET` apply to **RFQItems**, then joins the `hasMany` children outside. The existing internal path already sets `subQuery: true`, so this is consistent.
- `ORDER BY` columns from `belongsTo` includes (e.g. `RFQGroup_name`, `RFQItemStatus_title`) under `subQuery:true` must be referenced as `$Assoc.col$` via `Sequelize.col('Assoc.col')` or a literal, **and** the association must be `required`/joined inside the subquery. belongsTo includes are LEFT JOINed inside the subquery by Sequelize when ordered on — safe. Ordering on a `hasMany` (RFQVendors) directly is NOT allowed under subQuery; Res ordering therefore must use a **correlated scalar subquery in ORDER BY** (Section E), not an aggregate over the joined alias.
- **lastRow count:** use `sql.RFQItem.count({ where, include: <same belongsTo includes + vendor where>, distinct: true, col: 'rfqItem_id' })`. `distinct:true` + `col:'rfqItem_id'` yields `COUNT(DISTINCT "RFQItem"."rfqItem_id")` so `hasMany` fan-out does not inflate the total. Run concurrently with the page `findAll`.

**Per-row mapping:** pass `data` straight into `utility.remapRFQItem(data, merchGroupData, { merchGroupMap, whsecost })`. It produces the full client row including `Vendors` (master/detail keeps working, Req F #6) and `Res`. Return `{ rows: items, lastRow: totalLeaf }`.

**highlightedItem at leaf level:** after remap, apply the existing bump (`items.sort(a => a['Price Code'] == highlightedItem ? -1 : 1)`) within the page. (Cross-page bump is out of scope; current behavior only reorders the loaded array.)

**Vendor role:** when `user.role === 'vendor'`, `baseCondition` is the vendor variant (the `$RFQVendors.*$` conditions, `RFQVendor_archived`, division/active filters, `filterd_rfq` highlighted OR-branch) lifted verbatim from `getAll`. Vendor role does **not** group (grid grouping is an internal-role feature); the leaf builder is still used with `groupName` omitted. Keep `archiveStaleVendorQuotes` call and the `vendor.findOne` email backfill exactly as in `getAll`.

---

## D. Field→column map + two translators (in `helpers/rfqListQuery.js`)

A single source of truth mapping AG Grid `colId` → DB descriptor:

```js
const RFQ_FIELD_MAP = {
  'Price Code':   { col: 'rfqItem_style_number', type: 'text' },
  'Description':  { col: 'rfqItem_description',   type: 'text' },
  'Date Modified':{ col: 'rfqItem_date_modified',type: 'date' },
  'Created Date': { col: 'rfqItem_created_date',  type: 'date' },
  'Factory_updated': { col: 'rfqItem_factories_step_at', type: 'date' },
  'FOB Cost':     { col: 'rfqItem_fob_cost',      type: 'numString' },
  'Landed Cost $':{ col: 'rfqItem_landed_cost',   type: 'numString' },
  'CBM/pc':       { col: 'rfqItem_cbm_per_price', type: 'numString' },
  'Quantity':     { col: 'rfqItem_quantity',      type: 'numString' },
  // lookups (belongsTo): filter/sort by joined NAME
  'Status':       { assoc: 'RFQItemStatus', col: 'RFQItemStatus_title', fk: 'rfqItem_active', type: 'lookup' },
  'Step':         { assoc: 'RFQStep',  col: 'RFQStep_title',  type: 'lookup' },
  'Customer':     { assoc: 'customer', col: 'customers_name', type: 'lookup' },
  'Chosen Vendor':{ assoc: 'Factory',  col: 'factory_nickname', type: 'lookup' },
  'Depth':        { assoc: 'itemDepth', col: 'itemDepth_title', type: 'lookup' },
  'Delivery Location': { assoc: 'deliveryLocation', col: 'deliveryLocation_title', type: 'lookup' },
  'RFQ Group':    { assoc: 'RFQGroup', col: 'RFQGroup_name', type: 'lookup' }, // group col
  // merch-group (id-in columns)
  'Group':        { col: 'rfqItem_udf1', type: 'merch' },
  'Material':     { col: 'rfqItem_udf2', type: 'merch' },
  'Treatment':    { col: 'rfqItem_udf3', type: 'merch' },
  'Construction': { col: 'rfqItem_udf4', type: 'merch' },
  'Size (LxW)':   { col: 'rfqItem_size_l_w', type: 'merch' },
  'Res':          { type: 'res' }, // correlated subquery, Section E
};
```

**`translateFilterModel(filterModel, opts)` → Sequelize `where` fragment:**

1. **Multi normalization** (`agMultiColumnFilter`): the value is `{ filterType:'multi', filterModels:[m0, m1] }`. Normalize to the first non-null sub-model (`filterModels.find(Boolean)`), matching the requirement.
2. **Text** (`agTextColumnFilter`): support `contains`/`equals`/`startsWith`/`endsWith`/`notContains`/`blank`. Map to `Op.iLike` (`%term%` etc.) on the descriptor column. For `lookup` types, key on `$Assoc.col$` (e.g. `'$RFQItemStatus.RFQItemStatus_title$': { [Op.iLike]: '%x%' }`).
3. **Set** (`agSetColumnFilter`, e.g. Step/Status): `{ values: [...] }` → `{ [Op.in]: values }`. For lookups, IN on `$Assoc.col$` (the names the set filter shows). Handle `(blank)`/null members via `Op.or` with `{ [Op.is]: null }`.
4. **Date** (`agDateColumnFilter`): types `equals`/`greaterThan`/`lessThan`/`inRange` with `dateFrom`/`dateTo`. Map to `Op.gte`/`Op.lte`/`Op.between` on the DATE column. For `equals` use a day-range (`>= dateFrom AND < dateFrom+1d`) to be time-safe.
5. **Number on numString columns** (FOB/Landed/CBM/Quantity): the column is TEXT; cast for numeric compare. Use a literal:
   `Sequelize.where(Sequelize.cast(Sequelize.col('RFQItem.rfqItem_fob_cost'), 'double precision'), op, value)`.
   Guard non-numeric/empty strings so the cast does not error: wrap as
   `Sequelize.literal(`("rfqItem_fob_cost" ~ '^[0-9.]+$') AND CAST("rfqItem_fob_cost" AS double precision) ${sqlOp} ${num}`)`.
   (Regex guard avoids `invalid input syntax for type double precision`.) Support `equals/greaterThan/lessThan/inRange`.
6. **Res**: Section E.
7. **Merch**: Section F.

Combine all per-column fragments with `Op.and`.

**`translateSortModel(sortModel, opts)` → Sequelize `order`:**

- text/date/lookup → `[Sequelize.col(col-or-$Assoc.col$), dir]`. For lookups under `subQuery:true`, use `Sequelize.literal('"Assoc"."col" DIR')` to be safe.
- numString → `[Sequelize.literal(`CAST(NULLIF("col",'') AS double precision) ${dir} NULLS LAST`)]` (NULLIF so empty strings sort as null).
- Res → ORDER on the correlated subquery literal (Section E).
- Always append a deterministic tiebreak: `[Sequelize.literal('"rfqItem_id" DESC')]`.

**Lookup filter/sort by name vs FK:** filter and sort on the **joined name** (`$Assoc.col$`), because that is what the user sees and what set-filter `values` contain. This forces those belongsTo includes to participate in the subquery; Sequelize handles belongsTo (one-to-one) joins inside the subquery wrapper, so `LIMIT` on RFQItem stays correct. (Filtering by FK id would avoid the join but breaks set filters whose values are names — rejected.)

---

## E. Res subquery (responded & requested counts)

`Res` has no column. Define two correlated scalar subqueries over `RFQVendor`, correlated on the outer `RFQItem.rfqItem_id`, mirroring the JS logic in `utility.js` (requested = distinct `vendor_id_fk` where `req_status <> 1`; responded = distinct `vendor_id_fk` where `req_status <> 1` AND `RFQVendor_amount` is a non-null number):

```sql
-- requested
(SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT v."vendor_id_fk")
 FROM <schema>"RFQVendor" v
 WHERE v."RFQitem_id_fk" = "RFQItem"."rfqItem_id"
   AND COALESCE(v."req_status",0) <> 1)
-- responded
(SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT v."vendor_id_fk")
 FROM <schema>"RFQVendor" v
 WHERE v."RFQitem_id_fk" = "RFQItem"."rfqItem_id"
   AND COALESCE(v."req_status",0) <> 1
   AND v."RFQVendor_amount" ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$')
```

Provide `buildResSubqueries(schemaPrefix)` returning `{ requestedSql, respondedSql }` strings.

- **SELECT** (optional): expose as computed attributes `[Sequelize.literal(respondedSql), 'res_responded']` etc. Not required since `remapRFQItem` recomputes Res from the `Vendors` array, but useful for debugging/export header.
- **WHERE (filter):** the user types e.g. `"3/5"`. Parse the text filter term `"<r>/<q>"`; if both parts numeric, add `Sequelize.literal(`(${respondedSql}) = ${r} AND (${requestedSql}) = ${q}`)`. Support partial term `"3/"`→responded=3, `"/5"`→requested=5, and `contains` on the concatenated `responded || '/' || requested` via `Sequelize.literal(`((${respondedSql})::text || '/' || (${requestedSql})::text) ILIKE '%term%'`)`.
- **ORDER:** `Sequelize.literal(`(${respondedSql}) ${dir}, (${requestedSql}) ${dir}`)`. Correlated subqueries are valid in ORDER BY/WHERE and compose with `subQuery:true` because they reference the outer `RFQItem` alias which exists in the wrapping query. At the **group** level they likewise correlate fine inside the grouped where.

Use `process.env.schema` prefixing exactly like `getRFQGroup` (`schemaPrefix = process.env.schema ? '"'+schema+'".' : ''`).

---

## F. Merch-group filter translation

Columns Group/Material/Treatment/Construction/Size store **mg ids** in `rfqItem_udf1..udf4` / `rfqItem_size_l_w`. Avoid joining `merchGroup`; translate **names → ids** via the cached map (`getCachedMerchGroup` → `buildMerchGroupMap`, but inverted to name→ids):

```js
function buildMerchNameToIds(merchGroupData) {
  const m = new Map(); // lowercased mg_desc -> [mg_id...]
  for (const r of merchGroupData) { const v=r.dataValues||r;
    const k=String(v.mg_desc||'').toLowerCase();
    if(!m.has(k)) m.set(k,[]); m.get(k).push(v.mg_id); }
  return m;
}
```

- **Set filter** (`values: ['Cotton','Wool']`): map each value to its id list, flatten → `ids`; emit `{ [col]: { [Op.in]: ids } }`. Empty mapping ⇒ `{ [col]: { [Op.in]: [-1] } }` (no rows) so an unknown name correctly returns nothing. Include `(blank)` → `{ [Op.or]: [{ [col]: { [Op.in]: ids } }, { [col]: { [Op.is]: null } }] }` when blank selected.
- **Text filter** `contains 'cot'`: scan the name→ids map for descs whose lowercased text `includes('cot')`, collect all matching ids, emit `IN (ids)`. `equals` ⇒ exact desc match. This is feasible and cheap because the merch catalog is small and already cached (5-min TTL). Feasibility note: `contains` requires the in-memory scan (cannot push the substring to SQL without a join); acceptable given catalog size. If catalog ever grows large, fall back to a join on `merchGroup.mg_desc ILIKE` — but default is the id-translation path per requirement.

Because both translators need `merchGroupData`, the translator entry point accepts an injected `merchNameToIds` map; the model method fetches `getCachedMerchGroup` once and passes it to both group and leaf builders only when a merch filter is present.

---

## G. New/changed files & functions

**`helpers/rfqListQuery.js`** (extend; keep all existing exports):
- `RFQ_FIELD_MAP`
- `translateFilterModel(filterModel, { merchNameToIds, schemaPrefix })`
- `translateSortModel(sortModel, { schemaPrefix })`
- `buildResSubqueries(schemaPrefix)`
- `buildMerchNameToIds(merchGroupData)`
- `buildBaseCondition({ user, body })` — extract the existing internal & vendor `condition` construction out of `getAll` so both old and new paths share it (pure function; no I/O).
- `buildGroupLevelQuery(sql, ctx)` → `{ findOptions, countOptions }`
- `buildLeafLevelQuery(sql, ctx)` → `{ findOptions, countOptions }`
- `normalizeMultiFilter(model)`, `castNumStringWhere(col, op, val)`, helper `schemaPrefix()`.

**`models/lib.model.js`** — add `Customer.getAllPaged = async (req, result) => {...}` (surgical, per CLAUDE.md). It:
1. resolves user/token, builds `baseCondition` via `buildBaseCondition`,
2. reads `rowGroupCols/groupKeys`, decides level,
3. calls the matching builder, runs page+count (+ caches) via `Promise.all`,
4. group level → maps to group rows; leaf level → `remapRFQItem` + highlight bump,
5. `result(null, { rows, lastRow })`.
Leave `Customer.getAll` untouched.
- Add `Customer.exportAll = async (req, result) => {...}` (Req #7): same as leaf builder but **no limit/offset**, ignores grouping (returns flat leaf rows across all matching groups), reusing `translateFilterModel`/`translateSortModel` so export matches the current view. Returns the full `remapRFQItem` array.

**`controllers/main.controller.js`** — add:
- `exports.findAllPaged = (req,res)=> Customer.getAllPaged(req,(e,d)=> e?sendError(res,e,'SYS-0XX'):res.json(d));`
- `exports.exportAll = (req,res)=> Customer.exportAll(req,(e,d)=> e?sendError(res,e,'SYS-0XX'):res.json(d));`

**`routes/lib.router.js`** — add alongside the existing `getAllItem` (do not modify it):
- `router.post("/getAllItemPaged/", authRole([...same roles...]), customers.findAllPaged);`
- `router.post("/getAllItemExport/", authRole([...]), customers.exportAll);`

**Rollout/flag:** new endpoints live beside the old one; the Angular grid switches its datasource to `/getAllItemPaged`. Old `/getAllItem` stays as instant rollback. Optionally gate via an env flag (`RFQ_SSRM_ENABLED`) read by the frontend BFF, but the dual-endpoint approach already gives clean rollback without backend branching.

---

## H. Risks, Sequelize gotchas, test plan

**Gotchas / risks:**
1. **subQuery + hasMany + limit:** must keep `subQuery:true` on the leaf query or `LIMIT` cuts joined `RFQVendor` rows. Verified the existing path already does this.
2. **Count with hasMany:** must use `count({ distinct:true, col:'rfqItem_id' })`; a plain count over the joined query inflates by vendor rows. For group level use `count({ distinct:true, col:'rfqItem_rfq_group' })`.
3. **GROUP BY + findAndCountAll** returns array counts, not scalar — never use it; compute the scalar count separately.
4. **Ordering on aggregates / hasMany alias** is illegal under subQuery; Res ordering and numString ordering must use correlated subquery / CAST literals, not aliased aggregates.
5. **CAST on free-text numeric columns** throws on non-numeric/empty values; always guard with a regex (`~ '^[0-9.]+$'`) or `NULLIF(col,'')` before casting.
6. **Correlated subqueries in WHERE/ORDER under subQuery:true:** they reference the outer `"RFQItem"` alias — confirm the alias literal matches Sequelize's generated alias (`"RFQItem"`); the model's `tableName` is `RFQItem` and existing code already correlates with `"RFQItem"` in `getRFQGroup`-style literals.
7. **Schema prefix:** every raw literal (Res, merch fallback) must use `process.env.schema` prefixing like `getRFQGroup`, or queries break in the schema-scoped deployment.
8. **belongsTo include + sort/filter on name** pulls those joins into the subquery; verify generated SQL once with `logging` to confirm `LIMIT` still wraps RFQItem.
9. **Set-filter blanks and null FKs** (Status/Step often null) — handle `(blank)` explicitly or rows silently drop.
10. **Performance:** add/confirm indexes on `rfqItem_rfq_group`, `rfqItem_date_modified`, `RFQVendor.RFQitem_id_fk` (Res subqueries run per row). Flag to DBA; not in code scope here.

**Test plan** (extend `tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`, pure-function unit tests, no DB):
- `normalizeMultiFilter`: picks first sub-model; passes through single filters.
- `translateFilterModel`:
  - text contains/equals/blank → correct `Op.iLike`/null.
  - set → `Op.in` (+ blank → `Op.or` null).
  - date equals/inRange → `Op.gte`/`Op.lte`/`Op.between` with day-range.
  - numString → emits CAST literal with regex guard (assert generated SQL substring via a stub `Sequelize`).
  - lookup → keys on `$Assoc.col$`.
  - Res `"3/5"`, `"3/"`, `"/5"`, contains → correct correlated-literal predicate.
  - merch set (names→ids via injected map), unknown name → `IN [-1]`, blank handling; merch contains scans map.
- `translateSortModel`: text/date direction, numString CAST NULLS LAST, Res order literal, always-appended `rfqItem_id DESC` tiebreak.
- `buildBaseCondition`: internal vs vendor branch (division arrays, archive status, `filterd_rfq` OR-branch, highlighted) — assert structure matches the current `getAll` condition objects.
- `buildGroupLevelQuery` / `buildLeafLevelQuery`: assert returned `findOptions` carry expected `group`/`order`/`limit`/`offset`/`subQuery`/`distinct` and that the group `where` ANDs base+filter; assert count options use the right `distinct`/`col`.
- `buildResSubqueries`: contains both correlated SELECTs and respects schema prefix (set/unset `process.env.schema`).
- Regression: existing four tests stay green (no signature changes to exported helpers reused by `getAll`).
- Integration smoke (optional, if a test DB harness exists): one group-level request (`groupKeys:[]`) returns group rows + `lastRow`; one leaf request (`groupKeys:['X']`) returns leaf rows with `Vendors` populated and correct `lastRow`.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\models\lib.model.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\utility.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\controllers\main.controller.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\routes\lib.router.js

(Test file to mirror/extend: C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\tests\unit\rfqListQuery.test.js)

Note: I was unable to write the plan to `snazzy-hatching-balloon-agent-afb69c57dbd1b3f7c.md` because no Write tool is available in this read-only planning session; the complete design is delivered above.

content.text:
I don't have a Write tool available (read-only planning mode, and Write isn't in the deferred list). I'll deliver the design directly as my response, which is the correct output for this planning task.

---

# Design: Backend Query Layer for AG Grid SSRM with Server-Side Row Grouping (RFQ List)

This design adds a paginated, server-side-grouped, server-filtered/sorted query layer for the RFQ list while leaving the existing `POST /getAllItem` → `Customer.getAll` path fully intact for rollback. All new query logic lands in `helpers/rfqListQuery.js`; a thin model method and controller/route are added.

Confirmed facts grounding this design:
- Recency-ordered group query pattern already exists at `models/lib.model.js:1970-1986` (`SELECT MAX("rfqItem_date_modified") FROM "RFQItem" WHERE "rfqItem_rfq_group" = "RFQGroup"."RFQGroup_id"`, with `Sequelize.literal('"last_activity" DESC NULLS LAST')` and the schema-prefix idiom `process.env.schema ? '"'+schema+'".' : ''`).
- Tables: `RFQItem` (PK `rfqItem_id`, group FK `rfqItem_rfq_group` → `RFQGroup.RFQGroup_id`), `RFQVendor` (PK `RFQVendor_id`, FK `RFQitem_id_fk`, amount `RFQVendor_amount` STRING, `req_status`, `vendor_id_fk`).
- Aliases used by `buildRfqListLookupIncludes` are real (configured via UDFTable dynamic associations): `RFQVendors`, `customer`, `RFQItemStatus`, `itemDepth`, `deliveryLocation`, `RFQGroup`, `RFQStep`, `Factory`, `RFQContainer`.
- `remapRFQItem` (`helpers/utility.js:19-`) already builds the `Vendors` array and the `Res` string per row; it mutates `item.dataValues` in place, so it can be reused unchanged on leaf rows.

---

## A. AG Grid SSRM request contract (grouping)

The frontend datasource POSTs the standard `IServerSideGetRowsRequest`. The controller reads:

- `rowGroupCols`: array of grouped columns. For this feature it is always `[{ id, displayName, field }]` for the **RFQ Group** column (the UI is configured with `rowGroup: true` on that one column).
- `groupKeys`: the expanded group path. `[]` = top level (return group rows). `["GroupName"]` = inside one group (return that group's leaf rows).
- `sortModel`: `[{ colId, sort: 'asc'|'desc' }, ...]`.
- `filterModel`: `{ [colId]: <filter def> }`.
- `startRow`, `endRow`: pagination window (compute `limit = endRow - startRow`, `offset = startRow`).
- Plus our existing body fields preserved verbatim: `divisionCode`, `divisionStatus`, `status` ('archive' or not), `filterd_rfq`, `highlightedItem`. Auth header → `getUserInfo(token)` as today.

**Level decision rule** (mirrors AG Grid's own logic):

```
const groupingActive = rowGroupCols.length > 0;
const atLeafLevel    = !groupingActive || groupKeys.length >= rowGroupCols.length;
```

- `groupingActive && groupKeys.length < rowGroupCols.length` → **GROUP level** (Section B). Here `groupKeys.length === 0` and we return one row per matching RFQ Group.
- `atLeafLevel` → **LEAF level** (Section C). The active group is `groupKeys[0]` (the RFQGroup_name).

**Response shape (both levels):**

```json
{ "rows": [...], "lastRow": <number|-1> }
```

- `lastRow` is the total count at the current level (groups total for group level; leaf total within the group for leaf level). Return it whenever known so AG Grid stops requesting blocks; return `-1` when the returned block is full and we did not compute a count (we will always compute it via `COUNT(DISTINCT ...)`, so `lastRow` is always a real number here).
- Group rows must carry the **group key field**. Because the grid groups on the `RFQ Group` column whose value is an object `{ RFQGroup_name, RFQGroup_id }`, the cleanest contract is to make the SSRM **group key be the string `RFQGroup_name`**. Two supported approaches:
  - Configure the column's `keyCreator` / use `getServerSideGroupKey` so the group key equals `RFQGroup_name` (a primitive string). Group rows then only need `{ "RFQ Group": "<name>" }` (plus optional aggregated display fields like child count / last activity). `groupKeys[0]` arriving on the next request will be exactly that string.
  - `getRowId`: for **leaf** rows return the stable `id` (`rfqItem_id`, already emitted by `remapRFQItem` as `id`). For **group** rows, AG Grid manages group node ids from the group key; do not supply leaf `getRowId` logic for group-level rows (guard with `params.data?.id != null`).

So the server contract is: group rows = `{ "RFQ Group": name, childCount, lastActivity }`; leaf rows = full `remapRFQItem` objects (which include `id` and `Vendors`).

---

## B. GROUP-level SQL

Goal: distinct RFQ Groups that have ≥1 child matching the **same leaf filter**, ordered by most-recent child `rfqItem_date_modified` desc, paginated, plus a total distinct-group count for `lastRow`.

Do **not** use `RFQGroup.findAll` here (the existing `getRFQGroup` lists all groups regardless of children). Instead aggregate from `RFQItem` so empty/non-matching groups disappear and recency comes for free:

```js
// helpers/rfqListQuery.js  → buildGroupLevelQuery(sql, { user, baseCondition, filterModel, schemaPrefix })
const where = { [Op.and]: [ baseCondition, translateFilterModel(filterModel, /*excludeGroupCol*/ true) ] };

const queryOptions = {
  attributes: [
    [Sequelize.col('RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name'), 'rfqGroupName'],
    [Sequelize.col('RFQGroup.RFQGroup_id'),   'rfqGroupId'],
    [Sequelize.fn('MAX', Sequelize.col('RFQItem.rfqItem_date_modified')), 'lastActivity'],
    [Sequelize.fn('COUNT', Sequelize.col('RFQItem.rfqItem_id')), 'childCount'],
  ],
  include: [ /* only includes referenced by the active filter (see note) */ ],
  where,
  group: ['RFQGroup.RFQGroup_id', 'RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name'],
  order: [[Sequelize.literal('"lastActivity" DESC NULLS LAST')]],
  limit, offset,
  subQuery: false,
  raw: true,
  nest: false,
};
```

Notes:
- The `RFQGroup` include must be `required: true` so groups are joinable; FK is `rfqItem_rfq_group`.
- **Filter must apply to the same population.** Translate `filterModel` to a Sequelize `where` (Section D) and AND it with `baseCondition` (division/archive/active/vendor). Because a group with zero matching children produces no rows in the grouped result, "groups with no matching children disappear" is automatic.
- **Res / numeric-string filters that need correlated subqueries** (Section E/D) are added as `where: Sequelize.literal('(<corr subquery>) ...')` clauses; they correlate on `RFQItem.rfqItem_id` and work inside the grouped query.
- **lastRow / total group count:** a `GROUP BY` with `findAndCountAll` returns per-group counts, not a scalar — do **not** rely on it. Instead run a parallel `COUNT(DISTINCT rfqItem_rfq_group)` over the same `where`:

```js
const totalGroups = await sql.RFQItem.count({
  where, include: groupFilterIncludes,
  distinct: true, col: 'rfqItem_rfq_group',
});
```

Run the page query and the count concurrently (`Promise.all`), plus `getCachedMerchGroup`/`getCachedWhsePrice` only if merch-group filters are present.
- Map each row to `{ "RFQ Group": row.rfqGroupName, childCount: row.childCount, lastActivity: row.lastActivity }`. Return `{ rows, lastRow: totalGroups }`.
- **highlightedItem:** at group level, if a highlighted style number exists, optionally bump its group to top after ordering (find which group contains it via a cheap lookup). Simpler: keep recency order at group level and only bump the leaf row inside its group (Section C). Recommend the latter to preserve recency semantics — matches current behavior closest since `postSortRows` is the dominant rule.

---

## C. LEAF-level SQL

Reuse the existing findAll shape (`attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES }`, `include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(...)`), then add: group filter, translated filter/sort, pagination, and a correct distinct count.

```js
// buildLeafLevelQuery(sql, { user, baseCondition, groupName, filterModel, sortModel, schemaPrefix })
const where = {
  [Op.and]: [
    baseCondition,
    { '$RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name$': groupName },   // groupKeys[0]
    translateFilterModel(filterModel, /*excludeGroupCol*/ true),
  ],
};

const findOptions = {
  attributes: { exclude: RFQ_LIST_ITEM_EXCLUDE_ATTRIBUTES },
  where,
  include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes(sql, buildRfqVendorsInclude(sql, user)),
  order: translateSortModel(sortModel, { schemaPrefix }),  // + tiebreak rfqItem_id DESC
  limit, offset,
  subQuery: true,     // critical: see gotcha
  distinct: true,     // critical for count correctness
};
```

**subQuery / distinct correctness (the central pitfall):**
- `RFQVendors` (and the vendor-role `where` on it) is a `hasMany` include. With `limit` + a `hasMany` join, Postgres would multiply rows and `LIMIT` would cut joined rows, not RFQItems. Sequelize's fix is `subQuery: true`, which wraps the primary table in a subquery so `LIMIT/OFFSET` apply to **RFQItems**, then joins the `hasMany` children outside. The existing internal path already sets `subQuery: true`, so this is consistent.
- `ORDER BY` columns from `belongsTo` includes (e.g. `RFQGroup_name`, `RFQItemStatus_title`) under `subQuery:true` must be referenced as `$Assoc.col$` via `Sequelize.col('Assoc.col')` or a literal, **and** the association must be `required`/joined inside the subquery. belongsTo includes are LEFT JOINed inside the subquery by Sequelize when ordered on — safe. Ordering on a `hasMany` (RFQVendors) directly is NOT allowed under subQuery; Res ordering therefore must use a **correlated scalar subquery in ORDER BY** (Section E), not an aggregate over the joined alias.
- **lastRow count:** use `sql.RFQItem.count({ where, include: <same belongsTo includes + vendor where>, distinct: true, col: 'rfqItem_id' })`. `distinct:true` + `col:'rfqItem_id'` yields `COUNT(DISTINCT "RFQItem"."rfqItem_id")` so `hasMany` fan-out does not inflate the total. Run concurrently with the page `findAll`.

**Per-row mapping:** pass `data` straight into `utility.remapRFQItem(data, merchGroupData, { merchGroupMap, whsecost })`. It produces the full client row including `Vendors` (master/detail keeps working, Req F #6) and `Res`. Return `{ rows: items, lastRow: totalLeaf }`.

**highlightedItem at leaf level:** after remap, apply the existing bump (`items.sort(a => a['Price Code'] == highlightedItem ? -1 : 1)`) within the page. (Cross-page bump is out of scope; current behavior only reorders the loaded array.)

**Vendor role:** when `user.role === 'vendor'`, `baseCondition` is the vendor variant (the `$RFQVendors.*$` conditions, `RFQVendor_archived`, division/active filters, `filterd_rfq` highlighted OR-branch) lifted verbatim from `getAll`. Vendor role does **not** group (grid grouping is an internal-role feature); the leaf builder is still used with `groupName` omitted. Keep `archiveStaleVendorQuotes` call and the `vendor.findOne` email backfill exactly as in `getAll`.

---

## D. Field→column map + two translators (in `helpers/rfqListQuery.js`)

A single source of truth mapping AG Grid `colId` → DB descriptor:

```js
const RFQ_FIELD_MAP = {
  'Price Code':   { col: 'rfqItem_style_number', type: 'text' },
  'Description':  { col: 'rfqItem_description',   type: 'text' },
  'Date Modified':{ col: 'rfqItem_date_modified',type: 'date' },
  'Created Date': { col: 'rfqItem_created_date',  type: 'date' },
  'Factory_updated': { col: 'rfqItem_factories_step_at', type: 'date' },
  'FOB Cost':     { col: 'rfqItem_fob_cost',      type: 'numString' },
  'Landed Cost $':{ col: 'rfqItem_landed_cost',   type: 'numString' },
  'CBM/pc':       { col: 'rfqItem_cbm_per_price', type: 'numString' },
  'Quantity':     { col: 'rfqItem_quantity',      type: 'numString' },
  // lookups (belongsTo): filter/sort by joined NAME
  'Status':       { assoc: 'RFQItemStatus', col: 'RFQItemStatus_title', fk: 'rfqItem_active', type: 'lookup' },
  'Step':         { assoc: 'RFQStep',  col: 'RFQStep_title',  type: 'lookup' },
  'Customer':     { assoc: 'customer', col: 'customers_name', type: 'lookup' },
  'Chosen Vendor':{ assoc: 'Factory',  col: 'factory_nickname', type: 'lookup' },
  'Depth':        { assoc: 'itemDepth', col: 'itemDepth_title', type: 'lookup' },
  'Delivery Location': { assoc: 'deliveryLocation', col: 'deliveryLocation_title', type: 'lookup' },
  'RFQ Group':    { assoc: 'RFQGroup', col: 'RFQGroup_name', type: 'lookup' }, // group col
  // merch-group (id-in columns)
  'Group':        { col: 'rfqItem_udf1', type: 'merch' },
  'Material':     { col: 'rfqItem_udf2', type: 'merch' },
  'Treatment':    { col: 'rfqItem_udf3', type: 'merch' },
  'Construction': { col: 'rfqItem_udf4', type: 'merch' },
  'Size (LxW)':   { col: 'rfqItem_size_l_w', type: 'merch' },
  'Res':          { type: 'res' }, // correlated subquery, Section E
};
```

**`translateFilterModel(filterModel, opts)` → Sequelize `where` fragment:**

1. **Multi normalization** (`agMultiColumnFilter`): the value is `{ filterType:'multi', filterModels:[m0, m1] }`. Normalize to the first non-null sub-model (`filterModels.find(Boolean)`), matching the requirement.
2. **Text** (`agTextColumnFilter`): support `contains`/`equals`/`startsWith`/`endsWith`/`notContains`/`blank`. Map to `Op.iLike` (`%term%` etc.) on the descriptor column. For `lookup` types, key on `$Assoc.col$` (e.g. `'$RFQItemStatus.RFQItemStatus_title$': { [Op.iLike]: '%x%' }`).
3. **Set** (`agSetColumnFilter`, e.g. Step/Status): `{ values: [...] }` → `{ [Op.in]: values }`. For lookups, IN on `$Assoc.col$` (the names the set filter shows). Handle `(blank)`/null members via `Op.or` with `{ [Op.is]: null }`.
4. **Date** (`agDateColumnFilter`): types `equals`/`greaterThan`/`lessThan`/`inRange` with `dateFrom`/`dateTo`. Map to `Op.gte`/`Op.lte`/`Op.between` on the DATE column. For `equals` use a day-range (`>= dateFrom AND < dateFrom+1d`) to be time-safe.
5. **Number on numString columns** (FOB/Landed/CBM/Quantity): the column is TEXT; cast for numeric compare. Use a literal:
   `Sequelize.where(Sequelize.cast(Sequelize.col('RFQItem.rfqItem_fob_cost'), 'double precision'), op, value)`.
   Guard non-numeric/empty strings so the cast does not error: wrap as
   `Sequelize.literal(`("rfqItem_fob_cost" ~ '^[0-9.]+$') AND CAST("rfqItem_fob_cost" AS double precision) ${sqlOp} ${num}`)`.
   (Regex guard avoids `invalid input syntax for type double precision`.) Support `equals/greaterThan/lessThan/inRange`.
6. **Res**: Section E.
7. **Merch**: Section F.

Combine all per-column fragments with `Op.and`.

**`translateSortModel(sortModel, opts)` → Sequelize `order`:**

- text/date/lookup → `[Sequelize.col(col-or-$Assoc.col$), dir]`. For lookups under `subQuery:true`, use `Sequelize.literal('"Assoc"."col" DIR')` to be safe.
- numString → `[Sequelize.literal(`CAST(NULLIF("col",'') AS double precision) ${dir} NULLS LAST`)]` (NULLIF so empty strings sort as null).
- Res → ORDER on the correlated subquery literal (Section E).
- Always append a deterministic tiebreak: `[Sequelize.literal('"rfqItem_id" DESC')]`.

**Lookup filter/sort by name vs FK:** filter and sort on the **joined name** (`$Assoc.col$`), because that is what the user sees and what set-filter `values` contain. This forces those belongsTo includes to participate in the subquery; Sequelize handles belongsTo (one-to-one) joins inside the subquery wrapper, so `LIMIT` on RFQItem stays correct. (Filtering by FK id would avoid the join but breaks set filters whose values are names — rejected.)

---

## E. Res subquery (responded & requested counts)

`Res` has no column. Define two correlated scalar subqueries over `RFQVendor`, correlated on the outer `RFQItem.rfqItem_id`, mirroring the JS logic in `utility.js` (requested = distinct `vendor_id_fk` where `req_status <> 1`; responded = distinct `vendor_id_fk` where `req_status <> 1` AND `RFQVendor_amount` is a non-null number):

```sql
-- requested
(SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT v."vendor_id_fk")
 FROM <schema>"RFQVendor" v
 WHERE v."RFQitem_id_fk" = "RFQItem"."rfqItem_id"
   AND COALESCE(v."req_status",0) <> 1)
-- responded
(SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT v."vendor_id_fk")
 FROM <schema>"RFQVendor" v
 WHERE v."RFQitem_id_fk" = "RFQItem"."rfqItem_id"
   AND COALESCE(v."req_status",0) <> 1
   AND v."RFQVendor_amount" ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$')
```

Provide `buildResSubqueries(schemaPrefix)` returning `{ requestedSql, respondedSql }` strings.

- **SELECT** (optional): expose as computed attributes `[Sequelize.literal(respondedSql), 'res_responded']` etc. Not required since `remapRFQItem` recomputes Res from the `Vendors` array, but useful for debugging/export header.
- **WHERE (filter):** the user types e.g. `"3/5"`. Parse the text filter term `"<r>/<q>"`; if both parts numeric, add `Sequelize.literal(`(${respondedSql}) = ${r} AND (${requestedSql}) = ${q}`)`. Support partial term `"3/"`→responded=3, `"/5"`→requested=5, and `contains` on the concatenated `responded || '/' || requested` via `Sequelize.literal(`((${respondedSql})::text || '/' || (${requestedSql})::text) ILIKE '%term%'`)`.
- **ORDER:** `Sequelize.literal(`(${respondedSql}) ${dir}, (${requestedSql}) ${dir}`)`. Correlated subqueries are valid in ORDER BY/WHERE and compose with `subQuery:true` because they reference the outer `RFQItem` alias which exists in the wrapping query. At the **group** level they likewise correlate fine inside the grouped where.

Use `process.env.schema` prefixing exactly like `getRFQGroup` (`schemaPrefix = process.env.schema ? '"'+schema+'".' : ''`).

---

## F. Merch-group filter translation

Columns Group/Material/Treatment/Construction/Size store **mg ids** in `rfqItem_udf1..udf4` / `rfqItem_size_l_w`. Avoid joining `merchGroup`; translate **names → ids** via the cached map (`getCachedMerchGroup` → `buildMerchGroupMap`, but inverted to name→ids):

```js
function buildMerchNameToIds(merchGroupData) {
  const m = new Map(); // lowercased mg_desc -> [mg_id...]
  for (const r of merchGroupData) { const v=r.dataValues||r;
    const k=String(v.mg_desc||'').toLowerCase();
    if(!m.has(k)) m.set(k,[]); m.get(k).push(v.mg_id); }
  return m;
}
```

- **Set filter** (`values: ['Cotton','Wool']`): map each value to its id list, flatten → `ids`; emit `{ [col]: { [Op.in]: ids } }`. Empty mapping ⇒ `{ [col]: { [Op.in]: [-1] } }` (no rows) so an unknown name correctly returns nothing. Include `(blank)` → `{ [Op.or]: [{ [col]: { [Op.in]: ids } }, { [col]: { [Op.is]: null } }] }` when blank selected.
- **Text filter** `contains 'cot'`: scan the name→ids map for descs whose lowercased text `includes('cot')`, collect all matching ids, emit `IN (ids)`. `equals` ⇒ exact desc match. This is feasible and cheap because the merch catalog is small and already cached (5-min TTL). Feasibility note: `contains` requires the in-memory scan (cannot push the substring to SQL without a join); acceptable given catalog size. If catalog ever grows large, fall back to a join on `merchGroup.mg_desc ILIKE` — but default is the id-translation path per requirement.

Because both translators need `merchGroupData`, the translator entry point accepts an injected `merchNameToIds` map; the model method fetches `getCachedMerchGroup` once and passes it to both group and leaf builders only when a merch filter is present.

---

## G. New/changed files & functions

**`helpers/rfqListQuery.js`** (extend; keep all existing exports):
- `RFQ_FIELD_MAP`
- `translateFilterModel(filterModel, { merchNameToIds, schemaPrefix })`
- `translateSortModel(sortModel, { schemaPrefix })`
- `buildResSubqueries(schemaPrefix)`
- `buildMerchNameToIds(merchGroupData)`
- `buildBaseCondition({ user, body })` — extract the existing internal & vendor `condition` construction out of `getAll` so both old and new paths share it (pure function; no I/O).
- `buildGroupLevelQuery(sql, ctx)` → `{ findOptions, countOptions }`
- `buildLeafLevelQuery(sql, ctx)` → `{ findOptions, countOptions }`
- `normalizeMultiFilter(model)`, `castNumStringWhere(col, op, val)`, helper `schemaPrefix()`.

**`models/lib.model.js`** — add `Customer.getAllPaged = async (req, result) => {...}` (surgical, per CLAUDE.md). It:
1. resolves user/token, builds `baseCondition` via `buildBaseCondition`,
2. reads `rowGroupCols/groupKeys`, decides level,
3. calls the matching builder, runs page+count (+ caches) via `Promise.all`,
4. group level → maps to group rows; leaf level → `remapRFQItem` + highlight bump,
5. `result(null, { rows, lastRow })`.
Leave `Customer.getAll` untouched.
- Add `Customer.exportAll = async (req, result) => {...}` (Req #7): same as leaf builder but **no limit/offset**, ignores grouping (returns flat leaf rows across all matching groups), reusing `translateFilterModel`/`translateSortModel` so export matches the current view. Returns the full `remapRFQItem` array.

**`controllers/main.controller.js`** — add:
- `exports.findAllPaged = (req,res)=> Customer.getAllPaged(req,(e,d)=> e?sendError(res,e,'SYS-0XX'):res.json(d));`
- `exports.exportAll = (req,res)=> Customer.exportAll(req,(e,d)=> e?sendError(res,e,'SYS-0XX'):res.json(d));`

**`routes/lib.router.js`** — add alongside the existing `getAllItem` (do not modify it):
- `router.post("/getAllItemPaged/", authRole([...same roles...]), customers.findAllPaged);`
- `router.post("/getAllItemExport/", authRole([...]), customers.exportAll);`

**Rollout/flag:** new endpoints live beside the old one; the Angular grid switches its datasource to `/getAllItemPaged`. Old `/getAllItem` stays as instant rollback. Optionally gate via an env flag (`RFQ_SSRM_ENABLED`) read by the frontend BFF, but the dual-endpoint approach already gives clean rollback without backend branching.

---

## H. Risks, Sequelize gotchas, test plan

**Gotchas / risks:**
1. **subQuery + hasMany + limit:** must keep `subQuery:true` on the leaf query or `LIMIT` cuts joined `RFQVendor` rows. Verified the existing path already does this.
2. **Count with hasMany:** must use `count({ distinct:true, col:'rfqItem_id' })`; a plain count over the joined query inflates by vendor rows. For group level use `count({ distinct:true, col:'rfqItem_rfq_group' })`.
3. **GROUP BY + findAndCountAll** returns array counts, not scalar — never use it; compute the scalar count separately.
4. **Ordering on aggregates / hasMany alias** is illegal under subQuery; Res ordering and numString ordering must use correlated subquery / CAST literals, not aliased aggregates.
5. **CAST on free-text numeric columns** throws on non-numeric/empty values; always guard with a regex (`~ '^[0-9.]+$'`) or `NULLIF(col,'')` before casting.
6. **Correlated subqueries in WHERE/ORDER under subQuery:true:** they reference the outer `"RFQItem"` alias — confirm the alias literal matches Sequelize's generated alias (`"RFQItem"`); the model's `tableName` is `RFQItem` and existing code already correlates with `"RFQItem"` in `getRFQGroup`-style literals.
7. **Schema prefix:** every raw literal (Res, merch fallback) must use `process.env.schema` prefixing like `getRFQGroup`, or queries break in the schema-scoped deployment.
8. **belongsTo include + sort/filter on name** pulls those joins into the subquery; verify generated SQL once with `logging` to confirm `LIMIT` still wraps RFQItem.
9. **Set-filter blanks and null FKs** (Status/Step often null) — handle `(blank)` explicitly or rows silently drop.
10. **Performance:** add/confirm indexes on `rfqItem_rfq_group`, `rfqItem_date_modified`, `RFQVendor.RFQitem_id_fk` (Res subqueries run per row). Flag to DBA; not in code scope here.

**Test plan** (extend `tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`, pure-function unit tests, no DB):
- `normalizeMultiFilter`: picks first sub-model; passes through single filters.
- `translateFilterModel`:
  - text contains/equals/blank → correct `Op.iLike`/null.
  - set → `Op.in` (+ blank → `Op.or` null).
  - date equals/inRange → `Op.gte`/`Op.lte`/`Op.between` with day-range.
  - numString → emits CAST literal with regex guard (assert generated SQL substring via a stub `Sequelize`).
  - lookup → keys on `$Assoc.col$`.
  - Res `"3/5"`, `"3/"`, `"/5"`, contains → correct correlated-literal predicate.
  - merch set (names→ids via injected map), unknown name → `IN [-1]`, blank handling; merch contains scans map.
- `translateSortModel`: text/date direction, numString CAST NULLS LAST, Res order literal, always-appended `rfqItem_id DESC` tiebreak.
- `buildBaseCondition`: internal vs vendor branch (division arrays, archive status, `filterd_rfq` OR-branch, highlighted) — assert structure matches the current `getAll` condition objects.
- `buildGroupLevelQuery` / `buildLeafLevelQuery`: assert returned `findOptions` carry expected `group`/`order`/`limit`/`offset`/`subQuery`/`distinct` and that the group `where` ANDs base+filter; assert count options use the right `distinct`/`col`.
- `buildResSubqueries`: contains both correlated SELECTs and respects schema prefix (set/unset `process.env.schema`).
- Regression: existing four tests stay green (no signature changes to exported helpers reused by `getAll`).
- Integration smoke (optional, if a test DB harness exists): one group-level request (`groupKeys:[]`) returns group rows + `lastRow`; one leaf request (`groupKeys:['X']`) returns leaf rows with `Vendors` populated and correct `lastRow`.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\rfqListQuery.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\models\lib.model.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\helpers\utility.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\controllers\main.controller.js
- C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\routes\lib.router.js

(Test file to mirror/extend: C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\tests\unit\rfqListQuery.test.js)

Note: I was unable to write the plan to `snazzy-hatching-balloon-agent-afb69c57dbd1b3f7c.md` because no Write tool is available in this read-only planning session; the complete design is delivered above.

input.content:
# RFQ grid → AG Grid Server-Side Row Model (SSRM) with server-side grouping

## Context

The RFQ list (`designflow-backend` `Customer.getAll` → frontend `rfq.component.ts`) currently
loads **every** RFQ row (3000+) in one unbounded `findAll` across ~10 joined tables, then does
all filtering/sorting **client-side**. It's the heaviest page in the app and the biggest
page-load cost. The goal is a true SSRM migration: the backend paginates and applies
filtering + sorting + grouping in SQL so they operate across **all** matching rows, not just
the rows currently displayed.

Per the user's decisions:
1. **Keep server-side row grouping** by "RFQ Group" (collapsible groups, ordered by most-recent
   child "Date Modified" — matching today's `postSortRows`).
2. **Res and merch-group columns MUST be filterable** (and Res sortable). **Carton
   length/width/height** do not need sort/filter.
3. **Export must return the full filtered set** (server-side), not just loaded pages.

Rollout is **dual-endpoint**: new paginated endpoints are added alongside the existing
`/getAllItem`, which stays as an instant rollback path.

## Approach (recommended)

### Backend (`designflow-backend`)

All new query logic goes in **`helpers/rfqListQuery.js`** (extend; keep existing exports that
`getAll` still uses). New thin model methods + controller + routes. `Customer.getAll` is left
untouched for rollback.

**1. Shared base condition** — extract the existing internal-vs-vendor `where` construction out
of `getAll` into `buildBaseCondition({ user, body })` (division/archive/active filters, vendor
`$RFQVendors.*$` branch, `filterd_rfq`/highlighted OR-branch). Pure function, reused by old and
new paths.

**2. Filter/sort translators** + a single field map:
- `RFQ_FIELD_MAP`: colId → `{ col | assoc+col, type }` where type ∈ text | date | numString |
  lookup | merch | res. (Price Code, Description, dates → direct cols; FOB Cost / Landed Cost /
  CBM/pc / Quantity → `numString` (TEXT needing numeric CAST); Status/Step/Customer/Chosen
  Vendor/Depth/Delivery Location/RFQ Group → `lookup` on the joined name; Group/Material/
  Treatment/Construction/Size → `merch`; Res → `res`.)
- `translateFilterModel(filterModel, { merchNameToIds, schemaPrefix })` → Sequelize `where`
  fragment. Handles agMultiColumnFilter (normalize to first sub-model), agTextColumnFilter
  (`Op.iLike`), agSetColumnFilter (`Op.in`, incl. `(blank)`→null), agDateColumnFilter
  (`gte`/`lte`/`between`, day-range for equals), and numString (regex-guarded `CAST(... AS double
  precision)` literal to avoid cast errors on non-numeric text).
- `translateSortModel(sortModel, { schemaPrefix })` → Sequelize `order`, with numString CAST +
  NULLS LAST, lookups on `$Assoc.col$`, and a deterministic `rfqItem_id DESC` tiebreak.

**3. Res (computed, no DB column)** — `buildResSubqueries(schemaPrefix)` returns correlated
scalar subqueries over `RFQVendor` (requested = distinct `vendor_id_fk` where `req_status<>1`;
responded = same + `RFQVendor_amount ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$'`), mirroring the JS in
`utility.js`. Used in WHERE (parse the typed `"r/q"` term, support `"3/"`, `"/5"`, and `contains`
on `responded||'/'||requested`) and in ORDER. Schema-prefixed like the existing `getRFQGroup`
recency subquery in `lib.model.js`.

**4. Merch-group filter** — translate filter **names → mg ids** via the cached merch-group map
(`getCachedMerchGroup` + new `buildMerchNameToIds`), then `rfqItem_udf*/size_l_w IN (ids)` (no
join). Set filter = map each value; text `contains` = scan cached descs for substring → collect
ids. Unknown name → `IN [-1]` (no rows). Carton dims: no translator entry (sort/filter disabled
in the column config).

**5. Group-level query** (`buildGroupLevelQuery`) — when `groupKeys.length === 0`: aggregate from
`RFQItem` (not `RFQGroup.findAll`, so empty/non-matching groups vanish), `group by RFQGroup_id`,
`MAX(rfqItem_date_modified)` for recency `ORDER BY ... DESC NULLS LAST`, `limit/offset`, apply
the same translated filter + base condition. Separate `count({distinct:true, col:'rfqItem_rfq_group'})`
for `lastRow`. Returns rows `{ "RFQ Group": name, childCount, lastActivity }`.

**6. Leaf-level query** (`buildLeafLevelQuery`) — when inside a group: existing findAll shape
(`attributes exclude`, `include: buildRfqListLookupIncludes`), plus
`'$RFQGroup.RFQGroup_name$' = groupKeys[0]`, translated filter/sort, `limit/offset`,
**`subQuery:true`** (so LIMIT applies to RFQItems not joined vendor rows), and a
`count({distinct:true, col:'rfqItem_id'})` for `lastRow`. Rows go through the **existing
`utility.remapRFQItem`** unchanged → each leaf row keeps its `Vendors` array (master/detail
keeps working) and `Res`. Vendor role uses the leaf builder without grouping.

**7. Model + controller + routes**:
- `Customer.getAllPaged` (reads `rowGroupCols`/`groupKeys`/`sortModel`/`filterModel`/`startRow`/
  `endRow` + existing body fields; picks group vs leaf; `Promise.all` page+count+caches;
  returns `{ rows, lastRow }`).
- `Customer.exportAll` (leaf builder, **no limit/offset**, flat across groups, same translators)
  → returns full `remapRFQItem` array.
- `controllers/main.controller.js`: `findAllPaged`, `exportAll` (new `SYS-###` codes).
- `routes/lib.router.js`: `POST /getAllItemPaged` and `POST /getAllItemExport`, same
  `authRole([...])` as `/getAllItem`. Leave `/getAllItem` intact.

### Frontend (`designflow-frontend`)

Mirror the proven SSRM pattern in `pages/prod_tracking` (it already does `masterDetail:true` +
SSRM) and `pages/itemLibrary`.

- **`rfq.component.ts` gridOptions**: add `rowModelType:'serverSide'`, `cacheBlockSize:100`,
  `maxBlocksInCache:10`, `blockLoadDebounceMillis:500`, `serverSideEnableClientSideSort:false`.
  Keep `masterDetail:true` and `getDetailRowData: p => p.successCallback(p.data.Vendors||[])`.
  Keep the `rowGroup:true` on RFQ Group; configure group key as the **string** `RFQGroup_name`
  (`keyCreator`/`getServerSideGroupKey`) so `groupKeys[0]` round-trips. **Remove client
  `postSortRows` + autoGroupColumnDef recency comparator** (recency now comes from the server's
  group order). `getRowId`: leaves → `data.id` (rfqItem_id); guard group rows.
- **Datasource**: `createServerSideDatasource()` whose `getRows` posts
  `{ startRow, endRow, rowGroupCols, groupKeys, sortModel, filterModel, divisionCode,
  divisionStatus, status, highlightedItem, filterd_rfq }` to `/getAllItemPaged`, then
  `params.success({ rowData: res.rows, rowCount: res.lastRow })`; merge cell notes per page
  (`mergePreloadCellNotes`). Replace the `getAllRFQItem()` → `setGridOption('rowData', …)` flow
  with `setGridOption('serverSideDatasource', …)`. Division/status/highlight changes call
  `gridApi.refreshServerSide({ purge:true })` instead of refetching the whole array.
- **Service** (`main.service.ts`): `getAllItemPaged(request)` and `getAllItemExport(request)`
  (POST; not cached).
- **Column config** (`rfq.grid.config.ts`): set carton length/width/height to
  `sortable:false, filter:false`. Leave Res + merch-group columns filterable (now server-backed).
- **Default Step filter**: keep `applyDefaultStepFilter` (it sets a `step` set-filter model) — under
  SSRM it is sent to the server and handled by `translateFilterModel`.
- **Export** (`onBtExport`): call `getAllItemExport` with the current filter/sort model, build the
  file from the returned full set (warn/spinner while fetching).
- **Saved Views**: column-state restore is unaffected; filter-model restore now triggers a server
  fetch — fine. **Selection/bulk ops** (Multi Quote, Add Factories, Reminder, bulk status):
  `selectAll:'filtered'` only covers loaded pages under SSRM — keep row-by-row selection working;
  flag full-dataset "select all" as a known limitation (out of scope unless requested).

## Critical files

- `designflow-backend/helpers/rfqListQuery.js` (translators, field map, builders, Res, merch)
- `designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js` (`getAllPaged`, `exportAll`; refactor base condition)
- `designflow-backend/controllers/main.controller.js`, `routes/lib.router.js`
- `designflow-backend/helpers/utility.js` (`remapRFQItem` reused unchanged)
- `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` (gridOptions, datasource, export, refresh)
- `designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` (carton cols off)
- `designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` (`getAllItemPaged`, `getAllItemExport`)
- `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/...` (no change — reads `data.Vendors`)

## Tests / verification

- **Backend unit tests** (extend `tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`, no DB): `translateFilterModel`
  (text/set/date/numString/multi/lookup), `translateSortModel` (CAST+NULLS, Res order, tiebreak),
  `buildResSubqueries` (with/without schema prefix), merch name→id (set/contains/unknown→[-1]),
  `buildBaseCondition` (internal vs vendor), and `buildGroupLevelQuery`/`buildLeafLevelQuery`
  option shape (group/order/limit/subQuery/distinct). Keep the 4 existing tests green.
- **Frontend unit tests**: a datasource spec (`getRows` builds the right request, calls
  `params.success` with `{rowData,rowCount}` and `params.fail` on error) mirroring the
  prod_tracking pattern; carton cols are non-sortable/non-filterable.
- **End-to-end manual** (the real proof — run the app): group level returns recency-ordered
  groups; expanding a group lazy-loads its leaves with vendor master/detail intact; sort/filter on
  Price Code, dates, prices (numeric), Status, Customer, **Res**, and **merch-group** columns all
  act across the full dataset (verify a value known to be on a non-first page); default Step filter
  applies; Division switch refreshes; Export downloads the full filtered set. Confirm via DB row
  counts that filtering reduces totals (not just the page). Run backend `npm test` and frontend
  `yarn test` (note: `rfq.component.spec.ts` has a pre-existing `notesDataSource` type error,
  unrelated).

## Key risks

- Sequelize: `subQuery:true` required for correct LIMIT with the `RFQVendors` hasMany; counts must
  use `distinct:true` + `col`; never `findAndCountAll` with `group`; CAST on free-text numeric
  columns must be regex-guarded; correlated Res subqueries and all raw literals must use the
  `process.env.schema` prefix.
- Performance: Res subqueries run per row — confirm indexes on `RFQVendor.RFQitem_id_fk`,
  `rfqItem_rfq_group`, `rfqItem_date_modified` (flag to DBA).
- This is the most critical page; ship behind the dual-endpoint so `/getAllItem` is instant
  rollback. Largest-risk areas to verify hardest: group↔leaf expansion, master/detail vendor data,
  and that filter/sort genuinely span all rows.
